### Stale-data warnings

- Older tables listing 62, 63, or 65 trace failures are historical snapshots,
  not the current aggregate.
- Older AIZ frontier claims at `f3135`, `f14299`, `f14301`, `f15016`, `f15795`,
  `f16217`, and `f16944` have been superseded by `f19089`.
- The bottom duplicate/older `S2 ARZ2 Obj15 child SST slot` entry is useful
  history, but its note that "AIZ remains at f14299" is superseded by the
  newer top entries.
- A green focused test or guard suite does not imply the trace suite is green.
  The current suite state remains expected-red until a fresh full sweep says
  otherwise.

### Update protocol for future agents

- When a full `*TraceReplay` sweep is run, update `Agent Quick State` before
  adding the dated evidence entry.
- When a focused trace frontier moves, update the active queue and focused
  frontier table in this section.
- If a measurement is branch-local, dirty-worktree, investigation-only, or
  superseded, say so explicitly in both this summary and the dated entry.
- Keep the dated ledger append-only unless intentionally doing a documentation
  cleanup. Do not delete historical evidence only because it is stale.

## Evidence Ledger

## 2026-06-25 - S1 Walking Bomb shrapnel (Obj 0x5F) deletes via ROM obRender bit-7 render bound, not raw isOnScreenX(160); SBZ2 f1698 -> f2224 (957 -> 625)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 6e295f26a. One engine file: `Sonic1BombShrapnelInstance.java` (delete + keep-alive bound; `getOnScreenHalfWidth()=12` override).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Sbz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- Root (slot_dump-comparison method): the Walking Bomb shrapnel (Obj 0x5F, routine 6) self-deletes in ROM via `tst.b obRender(a0) / bpl.w DeleteObject` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5F Badnik - Walking Bomb.asm:218-219`) ‚ÄĒ the BuildSprites render-box on-screen test (X bound `[camX‚ąíobActWid, camX+320+obActWid)`, `obActWid=12` from `Bom_Main` `24/2`, same file line 28; `BuildSprites.asm:47-58`). The engine used `isOnScreenX(160)` (raw `[camX‚ąí160, camX+480]`), a much wider bound that kept shrapnel alive far longer than ROM (SLZ3/SBZ2 region: 24 live shrapnel vs ROM 16). The extra-live shrapnel occupy SST slots, shifting the FindFreeObj survival set so later shrapnel/objects land in different slots (SBZ2 f1698 was the prior swing-chain fix's new frontier; the over-lived shrapnel were part of the downstream slot drift).
- Fix: delete + `isPersistent()` now use `isWithinSolidContactBounds()` (the engine's ROM `obRender` bit-7 render-box helper) instead of `isOnScreenX(160)`, with a `getOnScreenHalfWidth()=12` override for the shrapnel's ROM `obActWid`. Object-local to Obj 0x5F shrapnel.
- After: SBZ2 f1698 -> f2224 (957 -> 625 errors); shrapnel live count in the SLZ3 region 24‚Üí15 (ROM 16). The new f2224 root is an unrelated 1px `x` continued-ride divergence.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 6e295f26a, first-error frame): ALL Walking-Bomb cross-zones clean ‚ÄĒ SLZ1 f2872, SLZ3 f3249, SBZ1 f6082 byte-identical (SLZ3 f3249's shrapnel-arc miss persists ‚ÄĒ a deeper per-shrapnel position residual, not the survival-count root). Every other S1 complete-run byte-identical: GHZ3 f8021, LZ1 f5745, LZ2 f1068, LZ3 f8499, MZ1 f2101, MZ2 f2823, MZ3 f2079, SYZ1 f4430, SYZ3 f6358, FZ f1724. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095 unchanged. `TestRewindCoverageGuard` 1/0 (no new coverage gaps).

## 2026-06-25 - S1 Swinging Platform / Spiked-Ball-Chain (Obj 0x15) allocates one OST-slot child per chain link (ROM Swing_Main .makechain); SBZ2 f1447 -> f1698 (977 -> 957)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 29dc29425. One engine file: `Sonic1SwingingPlatformObjectInstance.java` (+`SwingChainLinkChild` nested render-only child).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Sbz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- Root (slot_dump-comparison method, RAM-free): SBZ2 f1447 `obj_s69_slot exp=0x69 act=0x64` is a slot-NUMBER drift of the Walking Bomb (Obj 0x5F) ‚ÄĒ the bomb is present at the correct position, just in OST slot 0x64 vs ROM 0x69. Diffing the engine live slot map vs the ROM `slot_dump` aux (production-bootstrap probe, zone 5 act 1) at f1447 showed ROM has 9 Swinging Platform (Obj 0x15) objects vs engine 1 ‚ÄĒ a ~8-slot deficit (engine total 69 active objects vs ROM 76) that shifts the bombs and everything after down 5 slots. ROM `Swing_Main` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/15 Swinging Platforms.asm:55-105`) reads chain length from `obSubtype & 0xF` and allocates one SST slot per chain link via the `.makechain` FindFreeObj loop (`dbf d1`, d1=chainCount ‚Üí chainCount+1 links non-giant / chainCount giant); each link is routine $A (`Swing_Display`) render-only, positioned by the anchor's `Swing_Move2` (`asm:215-241`), no collision. The engine modelled the whole chain as a single object with internal `linkX[]`/`linkY[]` arrays and spawned zero OST-slot children.
- Fix: the anchor spawns `chainCount+1` (non-giant) / `chainCount` (giant) render-only `SwingChainLinkChild` OST-slot children via `spawnFreeChild` (FindFreeObj, matching ROM REV01 FixBugs=0), each positioned by the anchor each frame (`Swing_Move2`) and rendering its own link sprite (frame 1 chain / frame 2 anchor-end); the parent stops rendering the links (and its old separate pivot-anchor render) and keeps only the platform/ball render. Riding/solid/hurt are unchanged ‚ÄĒ they were always on the anchor. The off-by-one (engine first gave bombs slot 0x68, 1 short) was the `dbf d1` count: ROM runs d1+1 times, so the final `d3<0` anchor piece is a real child too (`chainCount+1`).
- After: SBZ2 f1447 -> f1698 (977 -> 957). Bombs now land in 0x69-0x6C; 0x15 count matches ROM (9). The new f1698 is an unrelated 1px `y` divergence (the RunningDisc/0x67 region noted ~250 frames further in).
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 29dc29425, first-error frame): ALL 4 Obj-0x15 zones clean ‚ÄĒ GHZ1/GHZ1CompleteRun/GHZ2 GREEN, MZ1 f2101/MZ2 f2823/MZ3 f2079 byte-identical, SLZ1 f2872/SLZ2 f3353/SLZ3 f3249 byte-identical. Every other S1 complete-run byte-identical: GHZ3 f8021, LZ1 f5745, LZ2 f1068, LZ3 f8499, SYZ1 f4430, SYZ3 f6358, SBZ1 f6082, FZ f1724. SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095 unchanged. Units: `TestS1SwingingPlatformSurfaceRegression` 1/0, `TestSwingMotion` 4/0, `TestRewindCoverageGuard` 1/0, `TestS1SimpleObjectGenericRecreate` 45/0, `TestSpawnRewindRecreatableCleanup` 46/0, `TestS1JumpFromElevator` 1/0.

## 2026-06-25 - S1 GHZ wrecking ball (Obj 0x48) starts swinging at boss state COMBAT_REVERSE (ob2ndRout==6), not COMBAT_MOVE; GHZ3 f7652 -> f8021 (119 -> 199)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` be7d19001 (includes the GHZ ledge + boss-bounce-halve + SLZ elevator fixes). One engine file: `GHZBossWreckingBall.java` (swing-start gate `>= 4` -> `>= 6`).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Ghz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- Before: GHZ3 f7652 `g_speed` expected=0x0297 actual=0x0000 (119 errors). The player (rolling, airborne) was spuriously HURT by the GHZ wrecking ball (Obj 0x48): engine rings 16->0, status 06->02, g_speed zeroed by the hurt. ROM keeps the player healthy (rings=16, status=06) ‚ÄĒ its ball was not overlapping. The g_speed=0 was a symptom of the spurious hurt, not a landing-conversion bug (the coordinator's framing was off ‚ÄĒ the player is airborne throughout, never landing).
- Root (disasm, no BizHawk): the player trajectory is byte-exact vs ROM through f7651 (pCy 036A == ROM 036A), so the player is not the problem. The ball's `getCollisionFlags()` is 0x81 = col_40x40|col_hurt; the engine's `decodeCategory(0x81)` correctly returns HURT (0x80 high bits), matching ROM `React_CollisionDetected` -> `React_ChkHurt` (col_hurt always hurts, no rolling protection ‚ÄĒ `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/Sonic ReactToItem.asm:182-189,335-360`). So the only way ROM doesn't hurt is a ball-position (swing-phase) divergence. ROM `GBall_Base` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/3D, 48 Boss - GHZ Main and Wrecking Ball.asm:506-511`) advances the ball to `GBall_Base2` (swing) only when the chain has fully extended AND the parent ship `ob2ndRout == 6`; the engine `extendChain` latched `chainFullyExtended` at `routineSecondary >= 4` (STATE_COMBAT_MOVE), one boss-state early, running the swing phase ahead of ROM. The swing math itself (ROM `GBall_Move`/`Swing_Move2`: `objoff_3E += 8` then `obAngle += objoff_3E`, side-flip at +/-0x200, init `objoff_3E = -0x200` side 0) is already faithful by inspection.
- Fix: `GHZBossWreckingBall.extendChain` swing-start gate `parent.getState().routineSecondary >= 4` -> `>= 6` (STATE_COMBAT_REVERSE), matching ROM `ob2ndRout == 6`.
- After: GHZ3 f7652 -> f8021 (119 -> 199 errors; frontier frame is the metric). The new f8021 root is an unrelated `y_speed` divergence (expected=-00A0 actual=0x0140) on a later rolling boss bounce at x=0x2A25 with the player healthy (rings=16) ~369 frames further in.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop be7d19001, first-error frame + count): change is object-local to Obj 0x48 (GHZ boss only). All other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1044, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/131, SLZ3 f3249, FZ f837/121. GHZ1/GHZ1CompleteRun/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged. `TestS1GhzBossGraphRewind` 3/0.

## 2026-06-25 - S1 boss-hit bounce halves the negated rebound velocity (React_BossHit neg/neg/asr/asr); GHZ3 f7279 -> f7652 (100 -> 119)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 1c6940005 (includes the GHZ ledge fix). Two engine files: `PhysicsFeatureSet.java` (new `bossHitHalvesBounceVelocity` flag + S1/S2/S3K constants) and `ObjectTouchResponseController.java` (unify the three boss-bounce sites through `applyBossBounce`, apply the halve).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Ghz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- Before: GHZ3 f7279 `y_speed` expected=0x020C actual=0x0418 (100 errors). The player (rolling, airborne, jumping LEFT into the GHZ boss Obj 0x3D, overlap ov=1) bounces off the boss; ROM x/y velocities at f7279 are (0x00D8, 0x020C), the engine's are (0x01B0, 0x0418) ‚ÄĒ each component is exactly DOUBLE ROM's.
- Root (disasm, no BizHawk): S1 `React_BossHit` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/Sonic ReactToItem.asm:259-263`) does `neg.w obVelX / neg.w obVelY / asr.w obVelX / asr.w obVelY` ‚ÄĒ it negates AND halves (arithmetic shift right) both velocity components. S2 `Touch_Enemy_Part2` and the multi-sprite boss path (`s2.asm:85318-85345`) only negate (no `asr`); S3K's `boss_hitcount2`/collision_property paths negate (and also negate ground_vel) without halving. The engine's boss-bounce only negated for all games (the comment cited S2 `Touch_Enemy_Part2`), so S1 boss rebounds were 2x the ROM speed.
- Fix: new `PhysicsFeatureSet.bossHitHalvesBounceVelocity` (S1 `true`, S2/S3K `false`). `ObjectTouchResponseController.applyBossBounce` negates then, when the flag is set, applies `>>= 1` (sign-preserving, matching `asr.w`) to both components before the existing S3K ground-velocity negate. The three boss-bounce sites (player ENEMY at the former inline negate, sidekick ENEMY, and the SPECIAL `applyBossBounce`) are unified through `applyBossBounce`.
- After: GHZ3 f7279 -> f7652 (100 -> 119 errors; the frontier frame is the metric). 0x0418 >> 1 = 0x020C (exact ROM match), 0x01B0 >> 1 = 0x00D8 (exact). The new f7652 root is an unrelated `g_speed` mismatch (expected=0x0297 actual=0x0000) ~373 frames further in.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 1c6940005, first-error frame + count): all S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1044, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/131, SLZ3 f3085/711, FZ f837/121. GHZ1/GHZ1CompleteRun/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged. Physics + boss units pass: TestPhysicsProfile 35/0, TestPhysicsProfileRegression 18/0, TestCollisionModel 10/0, TestSpindashGating 4/0, TestHTZBossTouchResponse 4/0, TestS1GhzBossGraphRewind 3/0, TestAizBossSmallInstance 1/0, TestS1Ghz3BridgeTerrainCollision 1/0.

## 2026-06-25 - S1 GHZ Collapsing Ledge (Obj 0x1A) clears the rider's on-object/pushing status on collapse-release; GHZ3 f6464 -> f7279 (271 -> 100)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 5b2d067d6. One engine file: `Sonic1CollapsingLedgeObjectInstance.java` (two setter calls in the collapse-release branch).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Ghz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- Before: GHZ3 f6464 `y` expected=0x038C actual=0x038E (271 errors). The engine seated Sonic 2px low on the GHZ CollapsingLedge slope (`st=08`, on-object) where ROM had `status=00` (on terrain).
- Root (disasm-tractable via the collision-ORDER lens, no BizHawk): the engine rode the ledge faithfully through f6463 (player Y matched ROM byte-for-byte). At f6464 the ledge fragment-collapse timer reaches zero and the ledge releases the still-standing player. ROM `Ledge_FragmentPiece .delayCollapse` does `bclr #3,obStatus(a1) / bclr #5,obStatus(a1)` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/1A, 53 Collapsing Ledges and Floors.asm:104-105`), clearing Sonic's `Status_OnObj`/`Status_Push` so `Sonic_DoLevelCollision` re-seats him onto terrain that frame (Y 0x038E -> 0x038C, 2px up ‚ÄĒ the ledge slope rides ~2px below the underlying terrain near the right edge). The engine's `updateFragmentFall` called `objectManager.clearRidingObject(player)` (drops engine-side riding bookkeeping) but did NOT clear the player's `isOnObject()` flag, so `CollisionSystem.checkGroundCollision` (the `if (sprite.isOnObject())` early-return at `CollisionSystem.java:435`) kept suppressing the terrain re-seat and the player stayed pinned at the ledge's last slope Y.
- Verified NOT terrain-data and NOT slope-data: an in-engine terrain probe at the seat column (x=0x213A) found the GHZ3 SolidTile at full height with the surface top at 0x03A0 (player feet land at 0x039F for centre 0x038C) ‚ÄĒ the terrain ROM lands on IS present in the engine; the SLOPE_DATA + seat formula are byte-faithful (per prior verification). The 2px was purely the missing on-object/pushing clear blocking terrain re-collision on the release frame.
- Fix: in `Sonic1CollapsingLedgeObjectInstance.updateFragmentFall`, the collapse-release branch (`collapseDelay <= 0` with the player still riding) now calls `player.setOnObject(false)` and `player.setPushing(false)` alongside `clearRidingObject(player)`, matching ROM `bclr #3/#5,obStatus(a1)`.
- After: GHZ3 f6464 -> f7279 (271 -> 100 errors). The new f7279 root is an unrelated `y_speed` mismatch (expected=0x020C actual=0x0418) on a jump/bounce ~815 frames further in.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 5b2d067d6, first-error frame + count): change is object-local to Obj 0x1A (GHZ-only spawn). All other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1044, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/131, SLZ3 f3085/711, FZ f837/121. GHZ1/GHZ1CompleteRun/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged.

## 2026-06-25 - S1 SLZ Seesaw (Obj 0x5E) rejects the exact-touch zero-distance landing (Obj18/circling-platform family); SLZ3 f1416 -> f3085 (1394 -> 711)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 97c699bca. One engine file: `Sonic1SeesawObjectInstance.java` (one override).
- Command: `mvn -o test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dtrace.context.diagnosticChars=full" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`. Baseline f1416 `y_speed` exp=0x09E8 act=0x0000.
- **Class: object/physics ‚ÄĒ seesaw landing detection 1 frame early.** At f1416 a rolling-jump Sonic falls fast (y_speed ~0x09E8) toward the Seesaw (Obj 0x5E @04C0,01F0; ROM stand_on_obj 0x25, engine onSlot 0x33 ‚ÄĒ SAME seesaw, verified via the SLZ3 aux). ROM keeps him AIRBORNE at f1416 (Seesaw rtn 0x02 `See_Slope`) and seats him at f1417 (Seesaw rtn 0x02->0x04); the engine seated him at f1416 (air=0, ride=1).
- **Root:** ROM `See_Slope` (routine 2) lands via `SlopeObject`, which falls into the shared `Plat_NoXCheck_AltY` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub PlatformObject.asm:128-152,52-66`). That band uses the UNSIGNED `cmpi.w #-16,d0 / blo` test, so `d0=0` (flush touch) is rejected ‚ÄĒ standable band `d0 in [-16,-1]`. The engine's `resolveSlopedContact` -> `resolveContactInternal` honours `rejectsZeroDistanceTopSolidLanding()` (the same gate Obj 18 + the circling platform use), but the Seesaw didn't override it (default false), so it seated the flush-contact (d0=0) frame one frame early.
- **Fix:** Seesaw overrides `rejectsZeroDistanceTopSolidLanding()=true`. NOT needed: the `obY-8`/`obY-9` `getTopLandingSnapAdjustment` split ‚ÄĒ the seesaw surface is the heightmap (`obY - heightByte`) in both the landing (`SlopeObject`) and continued-ride (`SlopeObject_AssumeStoodOn`, routine 4) paths, so detect and ride surfaces already match.
- After: SLZ3 f1416 -> f3085 (+1669 frames). The new f3085 root is unrelated ‚ÄĒ an SLZ Fan (Obj 0x5D, onObj=0x5D) `camera_y` 2px divergence far away (player x 0x12D5 vs the seesaw 0x04C0).
- MANDATORY full sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, first-error frame vs develop 97c699bca): SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/131 byte-identical. Every non-SLZ S1 complete-run byte-identical: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1044, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f766/135. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 + `TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless`/`TestSonic3kLevelLoading` unchanged. Units (79/0): `TestSeesawBallGraphRewind` (6), `TestSeesawBallGraphRewindTest`, `CollisionSystemTest` (54), `TestSolidRoutineProfiles` (13), `TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn`, `TestSonic1LargeGrassyPlatformObjectInstance`, `TestRewindCoverageGuard`, `TestCollisionLogic`.
- NOTE: `TestS1Credits04Slz3TraceReplay` fails at f448 `status_byte` exp=0x0008 act=0x0028 (1 error) ‚ÄĒ VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING on clean develop (reverted my file, rebuilt, same f448/1 failure), unrelated to this seesaw landing fix.
## 2026-06-25 - S1 FZ boss reads previous-frame camera for wait-exit + cylinder defers first extension one frame; FZ f766 -> f837 (135 -> 121)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-fz-cylinder-phase` off `origin/develop` 97c699bca (worktree `agent-ae2d8ca4e23e2f8ff`). Two engine files: `Sonic1FZBossInstance.java`, `FZCylinder.java` (both FZ-boss-only).
- Command: `mvn -o test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1FzCompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`. Baseline f766 `y` exp=0x05AC act=0x05AB (1px).
- Root (pinned via gfc-tagged diag vs aux `object_near` Obj0x84 cadence): the FZ cylinder-attack cycle ran ~2 frames ahead of ROM. Two causes. (1) **Wait-exit camera read.** ROM `BossFinal_Eggman_Wait` reads `(v_screenposx).w` from ExecuteObjects, which runs BEFORE DeformLayers/ScrollHoriz in the level loop (`docs/s1disasm/_inc/DeformLayers (REV01).asm:16-18`), so the boss sees the PREVIOUS frame's camera. The engine's `camera.getX()` during the boss update already reflects this frame's scroll ‚ÄĒ diag: engine wait-exit at gfc 0x120 (camX 0x2452) vs ROM gfc 0x121 (ROM reads gfc 0x11F's 0x244D at gfc 0x120, exits gfc 0x121). One frame early -> first cylinder select gfc 0x121 vs ROM 0x122. (2) **Cylinder activation-frame extension.** ROM `EggmanCylinder_Action` (routine 2) sets objoff_29, advances obRoutine to 4, but falls through to its body with objoff_3C still cleared to 0 (`clr.l objoff_3C`, `_incObj/85,84,86 Boss‚Ä¶asm:692`) ‚ÄĒ so the activation frame seats the cylinder at rest (aux: rtn 2->4 at f768 with Y=0x620, first extension f769 at Y=0x61F); the engine extended on the activation frame.
- Fix: (1) `Sonic1FZBossInstance.updateWait` compares against a tracked `previousFrameCamX` instead of the live camera, restoring ROM's ExecuteObjects-before-DeformLayers read ordering. This makes the wait span the ROM-correct number of frames naturally, so the prior `waitFirstFrame` seed-compensation hack (added when only the seed, not the cycle phase, was being corrected) is REMOVED ‚ÄĒ the seed is now 0x7F at the first select without compensation. (2) `FZCylinder` defers its first extension step one frame after `activate()` (`activationDeferred`): the activation frame runs the routine-2 Action body (extension 0, seated at rest), extension begins next frame.
- After: FZ f766 -> f837 (135 -> 121). The new f837 root is the roll-into-boss damage bounce: ROM `x_speed=0x300` (the `move.w #$300` hit-bounce at loc_19F6A) vs engine -0x00F8 (engine bounces one frame late at f838; cylinders ~5px more extended) ‚ÄĒ a deeper per-frame hit-geometry / cylinder-extension-cadence residual, NOT the wait/activation phase.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 97c699bca, first-error frame + count): both changed files are FZ-boss-only. All 18 other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1044, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/131, SLZ3 f1416/1394, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged.

## 2026-06-24 - Monitor (Obj 0x26) top-overlap releases a rising rider who JUMPED off it; SLZ3 f1187 -> f1416 (1055 -> 1394)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 414003281. One engine file: `ObjectSolidContactController.java` (`resolveMonitorContact` rising-rider gate).
- Command: `mvn -o test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dtrace.context.diagnosticChars=full" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`. Baseline f1187 `camera_y` (downstream symptom of player y/air).
- **Root (pinned by stack-tracing every air-clear):** the air-clear bypasses `setAir()` via the direct field write in `setAirAfterObjectHurtLanding()`/`setAir(false)`, reached from `ObjectSolidContactController.resolveMonitorContact:2746` -> `applyObjectLandingState`. At SLZ3 f1187 Sonic does a ROLLING JUMP off a Monitor (Obj 0x26): `doJump()` fires (headroom OK, `air=true`, `rollingJump=true`, `y_speed=-0x680`), but the Monitor's solid pass that frame RE-SEATS him (`resolveMonitorContact` `canLand` branch lands him unconditionally) -> air cleared -> f1188 grounded. ROM `Mon_Solid` -> `SolidObject` gates the top re-seat (`Solid_ResetFloor`/`RideObject_SetRide`) on `tst.w y_vel / bmi` (the `SolidObjectFull` upward-velocity branch `loc_1E198 moveq #0,d4 / rts`, sonic3k.asm:41625-41626), so a rising player crossing the top is NOT re-seated.
- **Fix:** in `resolveMonitorContact`, when `!sticky && y_speed<0 && isJumping()`, return no contact (no lift, no OnObj, no air-clear) ‚ÄĒ the jump's airborne state + position survive.
- **Why `isJumping()` and not raw y_vel<0 (the MZ1 regression + narrowing):** the pure y_vel<0 gate (ROM's literal `tst.w y_vel`) advanced SLZ3 but REGRESSED MZ1 f2101 -> f1260. At MZ1 f1260 Sonic FALLS (y_speed +0x1C0) and ROM LANDS him on an adjacent intact Monitor (air 1->0, onObj 0->1), but the engine's player has a transient y_speed=-0x1F8 at the monitor pass ‚ÄĒ a broken monitor's break-bounce flips his y_vel up just BEFORE the landing-monitor's solid runs (an object-execution-order issue: ROM's y_vel is still >=0 at that pass). Instrumented both: SLZ3 f1187 `jumping=true rollJump=true`; MZ1 f1260 `jumping=false rollJump=false`. Adding `&& isJumping()` (ROM `Status_Jump`) scopes the release to the genuine jump-off case and restores MZ1 to f2101/169. ROM's gate is y_vel-only; this is a strict-subset narrowing that does NOT mask the separate MZ1 break-bounce-ordering bug.
- After: SLZ3 f1187 -> f1416 (the new f1416 root is an unrelated Seesaw (Obj 0x5E) ride: ROM `y_speed=0x09E8 air=1 rolling=1` vs engine grounded ‚ÄĒ onSlot=0x5E).
- MANDATORY full sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, first-error frame is the metric vs develop 414003281): all 19 S1 complete-runs byte-identical first-error frame ‚ÄĒ GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169 (un-regressed), MZ2 f2823/1044 (+1 downstream count, same frame), MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/131, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f766/135; GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN, CPZ f3365/310, CPZ2 f2889, MCZ (act 1) GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 + `TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless` unchanged. Monitor + collision units PASS: `TestS2Ehz1MonitorBreakRegression` (4), `TestSonic1MonitorObjectInstance` (6/2), `TestSonic3kMonitorObjectInstance`/`TestMonitorObjectInstance`/`TestMonitorIconTiming`, `CollisionSystemTest` (54), `TestSolidRoutineProfiles` (13), `TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn`, `TestRewindCoverageGuard` (69 across the collision set + 43 across monitor set).

## 2026-06-24 - Edge-balancing suppresses look-up/down camera pan, computed current-frame (shared player-movement); SLZ3 f1167 -> f1187 (1056 -> 1055)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 2fc658d51. One engine file: `PlayableSpriteMovement.java` (look gate + new side-effect-free `computeCurrentFrameBalancing()` predicate).
- Command: `mvn -o test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dtrace.context.diagnosticChars=full" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`. Baseline f1167 `camera_y` exp=0x00D5 act=0x00D8.
- This is the documented safe path for the earlier SLZ3 f1167 bounce. Root (from that bounce): post-hurt Sonic lands edge-balancing on a Monitor (Obj 0x26) holding Down; ROM `Sonic_Balance` -> `Sonic_ResetScr` suppresses look-up/down (eases v_lookshift to $60, camera up), but the engine's look code (`doGroundMove`) isn't balance-gated and the balance state is computed in `updateCrouchState()` AFTER it, so on the first post-hurt frame the look code read a stale not-balancing state and ducked the camera ~5px down.
- Fix: gate the look-up/down on a CURRENT-frame balance read. New `computeCurrentFrameBalancing()`: returns false if left/right held (ROM only reaches the look/duck/balance block when not steering); else snapshots `getDirection()` + `getBalanceState()`, runs the existing `updateBalanceState()` detection, reads `isBalancing()`, then RESTORES direction + balance level. The authoritative balance/facing is still produced at the normal time by `updateCrouchState()` ‚ÄĒ so the `checkObjectEdgeBalance` facing side-effects cited for CPZ2/MCZ1 are fully restored and no other trace's facing/balance timing changes. When balancing, the look gate falls to the `Obj01_ResetScr` ease-to-default branch like ROM.
- After: SLZ3 f1167 -> f1187. The new f1187 root is unrelated: ROM `y_speed=-0x680, air=1, rolling=1` (a rolling jump) vs engine `air=0, rolling=0, y_speed=0` (still grounded) ‚ÄĒ a jump-vs-grounded divergence; bias is now correctly $60.
- MANDATORY full sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, first-error frame is the metric vs develop 2fc658d51): **cited blast radius all baseline** ‚ÄĒ S2 CPZ f3365/310, **CPZ2 f2889** (first-error frame baseline), MCZ2 f4485, **MCZ1/act1 GREEN**, EHZ1 GREEN. All 19 S1 complete-runs byte-identical first-error frame: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6082/207, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f3353/151, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f766/135; GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 + `TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless` unchanged. Units: `TestCamera` (26), `TestCameraRewindSnapshot` (3), `TestLookScrollDelay` (5), `TestPhysicsProfile` (35), `TestCollisionModel` (10), `CollisionSystemTest` (54) PASS. `TestSidekickCpuFollowParity` has 2 failures (`s2NormalFollow.../s2TailsFlyIn...UsesDirectCpuLeaderBeforeSettledThreshold`) ‚ÄĒ VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING on clean develop (reverted my file, rebuilt, same 2 fail), unrelated to this change.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 Ball Hog (Obj 0x1E) + Cannonball (Obj 0x20) ObjectFall velocity ordering; SBZ1 f6081 -> f6082 (330 -> 207); residual is a 1-frame-early Ball Hog launch
- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-sbz1-f6081` off `origin/develop` 15a5972a2. One commit, two engine files (`Sonic1BallHogBadnikInstance.java`, `Sonic1CannonballInstance.java`).
- **Classified the f6081 root (decode-first):** at f6081 the engine player is HURT by the Cannonball (rings 11->0, recoiling) while ROM is fine (`onObj=68` standing on a Conveyor, `rings=0x0B`). But the player CSV shows ROM *also* takes the hit ‚ÄĒ at f6083, not "never": ROM rings=0x0B through f6082 then recoils at f6083. So it is NOT a spurious hit; the engine takes the SAME hit 2 frames EARLY. The ROM `object_near` cannonball (slot 0x43) trajectory (x decreases 1px/frame) pins it exactly: engine cannonball at f6082 sits at @(0x1FAA,0x040E) == ROM's f6083 position ‚ÄĒ engine is a clean frame(s) ahead. This is family (b)-adjacent (projectile trajectory/launch), object-local and tractable, NOT slot-cadence.
- **Root + fix (committed):** ROM `ObjectFall` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub ObjectFall & SpeedToPos.asm:8-23`) reads the OLD `y_vel` for the position add, THEN adds gravity to `y_vel` for the next frame (one-frame-delayed gravity). The engine Ball Hog and Cannonball both did `velY += gravity` BEFORE the move (manual pre-increment + `moveSprite2`) ‚ÄĒ one gravity-step ahead, so the cannonball integrated its fall+bounce arc too fast (arrived ~2 frames early, ~12px off). Routed both through the existing ROM-faithful `SubpixelMotion.objectFallXY` (cannonball, X+Y) / `objectFall` (Ball Hog init fall, Y-only) helpers. After: SBZ1 f6081 -> f6082, 330 -> 207; the cannonball trajectory now matches ROM's shape frame-for-frame.
- **Residual (characterized, not yet fixed):** the new f6082 first error is a 1-frame-EARLY Ball Hog *launch*, not trajectory. Instrumented: the engine Ball Hog @(0x1FF0,0x040C) launches its cannonball at `v_framecount` (gfc) 5922 (trace f6016); ROM launches at gfc 5923 (trace f6017) ‚ÄĒ exactly one frame early. The launch fires when the Ani_Hog animation reaches frame 1 (step 20). Ani_Hog (`docs/s1disasm/_anim/Ball Hog.asm`) is speed 9 (10-frame steps), 21 frames, afEnd-looping; the engine `ANIM_FRAMES`/`ANIM_SPEED=10` and launch-edge logic match ROM. The 1-frame is therefore the animation PHASE since the Ball Hog's routine-0->2 (landing) transition, an animation-init/spawn-phase subtlety. Deferred to avoid regressing the byte-identical SBZ2 (also Ball-Hog-bearing); likely needs the exact ROM Ball-Hog landing frame (BizHawk) or an AnimateSprite-init first-frame-advance audit.
- **Full sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, frame+count vs develop baselines):** Ball Hog/Cannonball appear only in SBZ1/SBZ2, so **SBZ2 f1447/977 is byte-identical** and every other S1 complete-run is byte-identical (LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SLZ1 f2872/163, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, GHZ3 f6464/271, FZ f766/135). GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3/MZ1 GREEN. (SLZ2 f3332/105 and SLZ3 f1167/1056 are the CURRENT develop baselines from the SLZ-seesaw fix at develop tip 15a5972a2 ‚ÄĒ not touched here; the two changed files are SBZ badniks only.) Cross-game: S2 EHZ1 GREEN, S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged + must-keep-green 30/0. Units: TestSonic1BadnikGenericRecreate / TestS1SimpleObjectGenericRecreate / CollisionSystemTest / TestRewindCoverageGuard 100/0.
## 2026-06-24 - S1 SLZ Circling Platform (Obj 0x5A) joins the Obj18 PlatformObject landing family (full obActWid top-landing width + obY-9 ride / obY-8 detect + zero-distance reject); SLZ2 f3332 -> f3353 (105 -> 151)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 15a5972a2. One engine file: `Sonic1CirclingPlatformObjectInstance.java`.
- Command: `mvn -o test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dtrace.context.diagnosticChars=full" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`. Baseline f3332 `y` exp=0x0118 act=0x011D (ROM grounded on the circling platform; engine airborne+rolling, `eng-expected-onObj s35 missing`).
- **Class: object/physics ‚ÄĒ rolling-jump landing on a circling platform (Obj 0x5A).** Sonic was hit by an SLZ bomb earlier, rolling-jumped (g_speed preserved 0x0260, rolling+airborne f3300-3331), and arcs onto a circling platform. ROM catches+unrolls him at f3332; the engine fell ~3 more frames and caught him at f3335.
- **Diagnosis (instrumented, ruled out 2 red herrings then found the real root):**
  - NOT oscillation phase: computed the S1 OscillateNumDo sequence in Python ‚ÄĒ the engine platform position (osc8/osc9 = v8=0x2F56, v9=0x04E2) exactly matches ROM tick 0x0CFA, and ROM `gfc=0x0CFB` is the POST-tick counter (ROM runs `OscillateNumDo` AFTER `ExecuteObjects`, `sonic.asm:2988/3009`, so objects read the prev-frame osc ‚ÄĒ same as the engine). Platform Y is stable 0x0134 in both. So the platform is correctly positioned.
  - NOT pre-update platform position: tried `usesPreUpdatePositionForSolidContact=true` (ROM Circ_Platform runs PlatformObject before Circ_Types) ‚ÄĒ INERT, because the platform's Y barely moves frame-to-frame.
  - ROOT (found by logging the `ObjectSolidContactController` top-solid catch): the Y window was satisfied every frame f3331-3334 but the catch was rejected by `isWithinTopLandingWidth`. The CirclingPlatform's collision halfWidth is $18; the generic `Solid_Landed` narrowing subtracts $B -> $0D, so the new-landing X window was [relX d1 in [0, $1A)] which rejected the player at relX=8 (f3332) and only passed at relX=12 (f3335). ROM `Circ_Platform` passes `obActWid`=$18 straight to PlatformObject, so the standable width is the full $18.
- **Fix (mirror Obj 18 `Sonic1PlatformObjectInstance`, since Obj 0x5A uses the same ROM `PlatformObject` routine-2 detect + `MvSonicOnPtfm2` routine-4 ride):** `usesCollisionHalfWidthForTopLanding()=true` (full $18 landing width, no -$B narrowing); `HALF_HEIGHT` 8 -> 9 (model the obY-9 MvSonicOnPtfm2 ride surface); `getTopLandingSnapAdjustment()=-1` (recover the obY-8 PlatformObject first-landing detect/snap); `rejectsZeroDistanceTopSolidLanding()=true` (ROM's unsigned `cmpi.w #-16,d0 / blo` band is [-16,-1], rejecting the exact-touch d0=0 ‚ÄĒ without it the engine caught a frame EARLY at f3331). The width fix alone moved f3332->f3331 (1 frame early); adding HALF_HEIGHT=9 + snap=-1 + zero-reject landed it on ROM's exact frame -> f3353.
- After: SLZ2 f3332 -> f3353. The new f3353 root is a 1px `y` (exp 0x0120 act 0x011F) while the player RIDES the same circling platform (onObj=35) ‚ÄĒ a distinct sub-pixel continued-ride-seat (obY-9 MvSonicOnPtfm2) residual, not the landing.
- Regression sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, frame+count vs develop 15a5972a2): Obj 0x5A is SLZ-only. SLZ1 f2872/163 and SLZ3 f1167/1056 byte-identical. Every non-SLZ S1 complete-run byte-identical: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f6081/330, SBZ2 f1447/977, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f766/135. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged + keep-green PASS. Units: `CollisionSystemTest`, `TestSolidRoutineProfiles`, `TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn`, `TestSonic1LargeGrassyPlatformObjectInstance`, `TestRewindCoverageGuard` (72/0).

## 2026-06-24 - S1 SBZ spinning platform (Obj 0x69) releases the SolidObject latch on detach; SBZ1 f5532 -> f6081 (frame +549; new root is BallHog, not the platforms)
- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-sbz1-f5532b` off `origin/develop` 1bf821932. One engine file: `Sonic1SpinPlatformObjectInstance.java` (detach path). Committed. Frontier advances 549 frames; the whole spin-platform-row section (f5531-f6080) now matches ROM.
- **Root (instrumented):** at f5532 the engine kept the rider grounded (`st=08` Status_OnObj set, `ride=0`, both spinners non-solid). Decode-first ruled out terrain (raw `Blk256_SBZ`=0x0000 at the ground-sensor cells, engine ChunkDesc byte-identical). The cause is the SolidObject **latch**: riding a spinner sets `latchedSolidObjectId=0x69` + `latchedSolidObjectInstance` (instrumented: the @0x1C10 detach at lvlFC 5457 logged `latch=0x69`). `finalizeInlinePlayer` returns early when `latchedSolidObjectId != 0` (a guard for CNZ latch-and-own controllers, ObjectSolidContactController.java:962-964), so on detach it never cleared `Status_OnObj` and never ran the stale-support air transition ‚ÄĒ the rider stayed pinned to the now-non-solid platform and a same-frame ground check held him grounded.
- **Fix:** in `detachRidingPlayer`, after `clearRidingObject`, drop the latch for THIS instance only (`getLatchedSolidObjectInstance() == this` ‚Üí `setLatchedSolidObjectId(0)`), and REMOVE the `forceAirOnStaleObjectSupportLoss` call. ROM `.notsolid2`/`.notsolid` (‚Ä¶asm:124-130 spinner, 83-89 trapdoor) does `bclr #3,obStatus(a1)` + `clr.b obSolid(a0)` ‚ÄĒ clears on-object/standing but does NOT set Status_InAir. The rider goes airborne only on the NEXT frame, when his own grounded floor check (Obj01 precedes the platform in ExecuteObjects, so the engine's inline player-first path matches) finds no floor. Forcing air the same frame went airborne one frame EARLY (tested: f5531/141, air exp=0 act=1). Releasing the latch + letting the grounded check set air is the ROM-faithful timing.
- **After:** SBZ1 f5532 -> f6081. New first error f6081 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0200: the engine player is HURT (rings 0 vs ROM 11, recoiling) from a BallHog (Obj 0x1E) Cannonball (Obj 0x20) hit that ROM does not take ‚ÄĒ a distinct, unrelated hit-detection root well past the spin platforms. Error count 150 -> 330 is entirely the newly-reached BallHog cascade; the spin-platform-row frames are now clean.
- **Full sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, frame+count vs develop baselines):** latch release is gated to this instance and Obj 0x69 is SBZ-only, so every other S1 complete-run is byte-identical (LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f2898/121, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, GHZ3 f6464/271, FZ f766/135) and GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. Cross-game: S2 EHZ1 GREEN, S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged + must-keep-green 30/0. Units: TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn / TestSolidRoutineProfiles / CollisionSystemTest / TestRewindCoverageGuard / TestS1SimpleObjectGenericRecreate / TestS1BadnikGenericRecreate 126/0.
## 2026-06-24 - S1 SLZ Seesaw spikeball: FindNextFreeObj spawn slot + See_MoveSpike->See_SpikeFall launch-frame fall-through; SLZ3 f814 -> f1167 (1024 -> 1056), SLZ2 f2898 -> f3332 (121 -> 105)
- Branch/worktree: agent worktree off `origin/develop` 1bf821932. Two engine files: `Sonic1SeesawBallObjectInstance.java` + `Sonic1SeesawObjectInstance.java`.
- Command: `mvn -o test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dtrace.context.diagnosticChars=full" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`. Baseline f814 `y_speed` exp=-0x0A80 act=0x0000 (ROM launches the standing player UP when the seesaw spikeball lands on the far end; engine still riding, air 0).
- **Root (instrumented, two stacked ~1-frame ordering bugs = ~2-frame launch lag):** the player stands STILL at x=0x075F (right end of the seesaw) f805-813; the spikeball (a previous bounce) is in flight and lands at ROM trace f814, whose `See_Spring` sets `obVelY(player) = -obVelY(ball)`. Mapped the engine free-running counter to trace frames via the player position (offset 17933: engine f18749 = trace f816). ROM ball (slot 0x4E, aux `object_near`) launches rtn 0x08->0x0A at trace **f719** and lands at **f814**; the engine ball launches at trace **f720** and lands at **f816** ‚ÄĒ uniformly 2 frames behind, same descent velocity, so pure phase.
  1. **Spawn slot order (1 frame):** ROM `See_Main` uses `FindNextFreeObj` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5E SLZ Seesaw.asm:38`) -> ball slot 0x78 ABOVE seesaw slot 0x70; ExecuteObjects runs the seesaw first, so the ball's `See_MoveSpike` reads the seesaw's just-updated `see_frame`. Engine used `spawnFreeChild` (FindFreeObj, lowest free) -> ball slot 0x53 BELOW seesaw slot 0x69, so the ball read the PREVIOUS frame's tilt target and launched one frame late. Fixed by switching the seesaw's ball spawn to `spawnChild` (FindNextFreeObj).
  2. **Launch-frame fall-through (1 frame):** ROM `See_MoveSpike` ends `bra.s See_SpikeFall` (`...asm:168-169`) so the ball runs `See_SpikeFall` (ObjectFall, plus the ascending double-gravity when `obY >= origY-0x2F`) on the SAME tick ‚ÄĒ ROM moves the ball 0x02F8->0x02E2 (~22px = 2 ObjectFalls) on the launch frame. Engine `updateResting` set velocity + `state=FLYING` and RETURNED (ball stationary that frame). Fixed by calling `updateFlying()` at the end of `updateResting()` after the state transition.
- After: SLZ3 f814 -> f1167 (the new f1167 root is an unrelated `camera_y` 3px tracking divergence with rings/0x37 sparkles + 0x5F objects, not the seesaw). BONUS: SLZ2 f2898 -> f3332 ‚ÄĒ the prior SLZ2 f2898 frontier (logged as "unrelated launch/bounce `y_speed` exp=-0x0A80 act=0x0000") was the SAME seesaw spring-launch bug.
- Regression sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, frame+count vs develop 1bf821932): seesaw is SLZ-only. SLZ1 f2872/163 byte-identical. Every non-SLZ S1 complete-run byte-identical: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f5532/150, SBZ2 f1447/977, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f766/135. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged + keep-green (`TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless`, `TestSonic3kLevelLoading`, `TestSonic3kBootstrapResolver`, `TestSonic3kDecodingUtils`) PASS. Units: `TestSeesawBallGraphRewind`, `TestSeesawBallGraphRewindTest`, `TestObjectManagerChildSlotAllocation`, `TestSlotAllocator`, `TestRewindCoverageGuard` (22/0).

## 2026-06-24 - S1 SBZ spinning platform (Obj 0x69) v_framecount spin gate + ROM-faithful detach-to-air; SBZ1 f5530 -> f5532 (387 -> 150)
- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-sbz1-f5532` off `origin/develop` ba512311c. One engine file: `Sonic1SpinPlatformObjectInstance.java` (two changes). Committed; clean net-positive win on both metrics (frame +2, errors -237).
- **Two ROM-grounded fixes, both verified by instrumentation:**
  1. **Spin gate v_framecount routing.** ROM `Spin_Spinner` gates the spin on `(v_framecount).w` (docs/s1disasm/_incObj/69 SBZ Spinning Platforms and Trapdoors.asm:96) but `ObjectManager.executeObjectWithSolidContext` passes the VBla clock into `update()`, running the cycle ~14 frames out of phase. Routed through `LevelManager.getFrameCounter()+1` (Electrocuter f1925 pattern). Instrumented: the engine spinner's `obj_frame` now matches ROM frame-for-frame for the platforms the player rides (@0x1C10 slot 0x52 flat at resolved-vfc 5456-5457 == ROM; @0x1C30 slot 0x55 flat at 5462 == ROM land). The coordinator's routine-0-defer angle was unnecessary: ROM `Spin_Main` falls through to `Spin_Spinner` via `bra.s` on the spawn frame, and the engine's spawn-frame AnimateSprite reset already holds frame 0 for 2 frames like ROM.
  2. **Detach-to-air.** ROM `.notsolid2`/`.notsolid` does `bclr #3,obStatus(a1)` (clears the rider's standing bit) so the rider falls when the platform stops being solid (‚Ä¶asm:124-130 spinner, 83-89 trapdoor). The engine's `detachRidingPlayer` only called `clearRidingObject` (removes the ride record); `finalizeInlinePlayer` then set `onObject(false)` but left `air` unchanged because no stale-support flag was queued, so the rider stayed grounded a frame too long. Added `objectManager.forceAirOnStaleObjectSupportLoss(player)` after `clearRidingObject`, which makes `finalizeInlinePlayer` set `air=true` on the support-loss frame. This is what dropped the count from 521 (gate-only) to 150.
- **f5532 root ruled NOT terrain (raw-ROM decode, same method as the f3971 pass):** decoded the raw `Blk256_SBZ` word + `Col_SBZ` at the player's f5532 ground-sensor cells (~0x1C19/0x1C2B, 0x49F) ‚ÄĒ `s1Word=0x0000`, `Col_SBZ[0]=0`, blank; the engine's `ChunkDesc` is byte-identical (also blank). So the engine's earlier `tile=00FF` grounded-ness was object detach-ordering, not terrain ‚ÄĒ outcome (c). The fix models the detach faithfully.
- **After:** SBZ1 f5530 -> f5532, 387 -> 150. New first error f5532 `air` exp=1 act=0 is a residual 1px object-detach landing detail (the rider goes airborne but a same-frame floor/landing check at the spinner-top level re-grounds him; both spinners and terrain are non-solid there, so it is a narrow detach-vs-land ordering residual, not the spin phase).
- **Full sweep (per-class, separate JVMs, frame+count vs develop baselines):** SBZ-Obj-0x69-only, so every other S1 complete-run is byte-identical (LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f2898/121, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, GHZ3 f6464/271, FZ f766/135) and GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. Cross-game: S2 EHZ1 GREEN, S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged + must-keep-green 30/0. Units: TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn / TestSolidRoutineProfiles / CollisionSystemTest / TestRewindCoverageGuard / TestS1SimpleObjectGenericRecreate 114/0.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 SBZ SpinConvey (Obj 0x6F) clears its v_obj63 spawner latch on out_of_range so the cluster re-spawns; SBZ1 f3971 -> f5530 (560 -> 387)
- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-sbz1-respawn` off `origin/develop` bc5647d26. One engine file: `Sonic1SpinConveyorObjectInstance.java` (`onUnload()` override).
- **Determination first (decode ruled out, BizHawk-free):** decoded the raw ROM `Blk256_SBZ` word at the player's right-sensor cell (world 0xF20,0x5A0) = `s1Word=0x0115` with ZERO solidity bits ($D/$E/$F clear) and `Col_SBZ[0x115]=0`; the engine's converted `ChunkDesc` is byte-perfect. The f3971 right "wall" is the SpinConvey (Obj 0x6F) group3 cluster (`SpinC_Data .group3` baseX=0xF80), NOT terrain. Corrects the prior log claim that f3971 was a terrain wall / not a conveyor root.
- **Root + fix:** the engine despawns the group3 cluster out_of_range at ~f3600 (baseX 0xF80 vs ROM camera 0x10D5; matches ROM) but never re-spawned it. ROM `object_near` slot history proves re-spawn: the group3 platforms vanish ~f3550-3650 and reappear in FRESH slots (37,47,50,71,83) by f3750. The engine modelled ROM `SpinC_OutOfRange`'s `bclr #0,(v_obj63,slot)` as `Sonic1ConveyorState.clearSpawned()` but never called it (dead code). Fix: `Sonic1SpinConveyorObjectInstance.onUnload()` clears `clearSpawned(pathIndex)` when a PLATFORM-mode child leaves the window via the counter-based out_of_range path (gated `mode==PLATFORM && initialized && !isDestroyed()` so the spawner's own post-spawn self-delete does not clear it). All of a spawner's 8 children share the same group baseX (the out_of_range reference is `objoff_30`=baseX, not live X), so they leave together; spawner slot maps 1:1 to child `pathIndex` (sub 0x80+N -> children 0xN0-0xN3). ROM ref: docs/s1disasm/_incObj/6F SBZ Spin Platform Conveyor.asm:5-31,37-152,234-279.
- **After:** SBZ1 f3971 -> f5530, 560 -> 387. New frontier f5530 `air` exp=0 act=1: player at x~0x1C16 running right; nearby objects are Obj 0x69 (SBZ Spinning Platform, slots 82/85/104/105 routine 4) -- a DISTINCT downstream root (0x69 ride/walk-off), not 0x6F.
- **Full regression sweep (per-class, separate JVMs; frame+count vs develop baselines).** Sibling Obj 0x63 LZ conveyor UNCHANGED: LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525 byte-identical. Release greens GREEN: GHZ1, GHZ2, SYZ2, SBZ3. Every other S1 complete-run byte-identical: GHZ3 f6464/271, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f2898/121, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f766/135 (FZ has zero 0x6F objects; matches the develop FZ baseline). Cross-game: S2 EHZ1 GREEN; S3K AIZ complete-run + must-keep-green (`TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless` 8/0, `TestSonic3kLevelLoading` 5/0+30/0) PASS. Units: CollisionSystemTest 54/0, TestSolidRoutineProfiles 13/0, TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn 1/0, TestRewindCoverageGuard 1/0 (no new gap).

## 2026-06-24 - S1 FZ boss adds 7 to v_random high word on every cylinder-attack side-contact frame (loc_19F50); FZ f743 -> f766 (156 -> 135)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-fz-plasma-trajectory` off `origin/develop` 74af905b6 (worktree `agent-ae2d8ca4e23e2f8ff`).
- Command: `mvn -o test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1FzCompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f743 `y_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0x0700. ROM Sonic stands still; engine jumps because a FZ plasma-ball (Obj0x86) overlaps him where ROM's balls sit elsewhere.
- Root (verified via aux `object_near` Obj0x86 trajectories vs an engine per-ball diag): the first plasma round's ball drop endpoints diverged ‚ÄĒ ROM `0x2564/0x251E/0x24C2/0x2476` vs engine `0x2565/0x2527/0x24D5/0x246F` (ball 0 matched, errors grew with spread: +1/+9/+19/-7). The drop/overshoot math (`BossPlasma_Spread`/`_Drop` incl. the shipped FixBugs=0 `add.w d0,obX` buggy-leftmost overshoot) and `targetX = boss_fz_x+$128 + objoff_32*-$4F + (rand&$1F) - $10` were already ROM-faithful and ball 0 confirmed the RNG matched at draw time ‚ÄĒ so the divergence was the **seed value** entering `BossPlasma_MakeBalls`. ROM `BossPlasma_Eggman_Crush`/`loc_19F50` runs `addq.w #7,(v_random).w` EVERY frame the boss `SolidObject` returns a side collision (d4==1, `bgt.s loc_19F50`, before the rolling/bounce check), `_incObj/85,84,86 Boss - FZ Main, Cylinders, and Plasma Balls.asm:192-195`. While Sonic rolls into the boss body during the cylinder attack (trace f360-408, roll=1 air=1), this advances v_random several times before the plasma round; the engine omitted it, so the ball-spread RNG draws used a stale seed.
- Fix (`Sonic1FZBossInstance.onSolidContact`, FZ-boss-only): on the `contact.touchSide()` path, before the rolling/bounce check, add 7 to the **high word** of the 32-bit RNG seed ‚ÄĒ `addq.w #7,(v_random).w` targets the big-endian high word (`(v_random).w`), a 16-bit add with no carry into the low word (`sub RandomNumber.asm` reads the full long; `sub SolidObject.asm:13` defines d4==1 as side collision).
- After: FZ f743 -> f766 (156 -> 135). New frontier f766 `y` exp=0x05AC act=0x05AB (1px): Sonic rides FZ Cylinder 0 (subpixels match ROM) but the cylinder/boss group is 1px high and the boss tracks a different host cylinder (ROM boss @2550 subtype-2 vs engine @24D0 subtype-0) ‚ÄĒ a distinct cylinder-attack 1px-Y / host-cylinder-selection residual, NOT the plasma RNG.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 74af905b6, first-error frame + count): change is entirely inside `Sonic1FZBossInstance` (FZ-only). All 18 other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f3971/560, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f2898/121, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 unchanged.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 FZ boss wait-state advances v_random every frame (BossFinal_Eggman_Wait loc_19EA2 + level-event-ordering spawn compensation); FZ f713 -> f743 (155 -> 156)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-fz-boss-graph-rng` off `origin/develop` ffe354e24 (worktree `agent-ae2d8ca4e23e2f8ff`).
- Command: `mvn -o test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1FzCompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f713 `y_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0x0700. ROM Sonic stands still mid-boss; engine jumps. Root is downstream of the FZ boss RNG: the first cylinder-select `RandomNumber` draw consumed the wrong `v_random` seed, so the wrong cylinder pair / plasma spread attacked the player.
- Root (verified by per-frame seed diag): ROM `BossFinal_Eggman_Wait` runs `addq.l #1,(v_random).w` at loc_19EA2 EVERY frame the boss is in the wait sub-state (fall-through tail, `_incObj/85,84,86 Boss - FZ Main, Cylinders, and Plasma Balls.asm`:131-133), advancing v_random through the boss-intro wait so the later cylinder-select draw picks the ROM spread. The engine omitted this entirely (seed stayed 0). ROM wait spans 127 frames (camera-gated; the FZ-boss PLC of 501 tiles @3/frame ‚Čą 168 frames finishes before the camera reaches boss_fz_x, so the camera is the binding wait-exit constraint and the `tst.l (v_plc_buffer)` gate never extends it ‚ÄĒ our synchronous art pipeline matches). A bump every wait frame alone produced 126 (seed 0x7E) and regressed to f296: the engine spawns the FZ boss ONE frame late because ROM runs `DynamicLevelEvents` AFTER `ScrollHoriz` in `DeformLayers` (`_inc/DeformLayers (REV01).asm`:16-18 ‚ÄĒ DLE reads the post-scroll camera) while the engine runs level events BEFORE the camera scroll (`LevelFrameStep` step 4 vs step 5 ‚ÄĒ `updateFZBoss` reads the pre-scroll/previous-frame camera), costing exactly one wait-state bump. The boss object's own wait-exit read is pre-scroll in BOTH (ExecuteObjects before DeformLayers), so only the spawn shifts.
- Fix (`Sonic1FZBossInstance.updateWait`, FZ-boss-only): bump `v_random` every wait frame (ROM loc_19EA2), plus a one-shot `waitFirstFrame` compensating bump that replays the wait-frame the engine loses to the pre-scroll level-event spawn read. Yields seed 0x7F (127 advances) at the first cylinder select, matching ROM.
- After: FZ f713 -> f743 (155 -> 156). New frontier f743 `y_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0x0700: a FZ plasma-ball (Obj0x86) at @24E2,05C6 overlaps the player (`touch s42 0x86 HURT ov=1`) where ROM's balls sit at @24AC/24D7/250B/2533,05C5 (rtn=08). The seed is now ROM-correct, so the balls launch from the right slots; the residual is the per-ball spread/drop trajectory geometry (`BossPlasma_MakeBalls`/`_Spread`/`_Drop`/`_Move`, incl. the FixBugs=0 `muls #-$4F` and `add.w d0,obX` buggy-leftmost behaviors) in `FZPlasmaLauncher`/`FZPlasmaBall` ‚ÄĒ a distinct deeper plasma-ball-AI root, NOT the wait-state RNG. The +1 total error is downstream plasma combat re-clustering; the frontier advanced 30 frames.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop ffe354e24, first-error frame + count): change is entirely inside `Sonic1FZBossInstance` (FZ-only). All 18 other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f8499/1525, MZ1 f2101/169, MZ2 f2823/1043, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f3971/560, SBZ2 f1447/977, SLZ1 f2872/163, SLZ2 f2898/121, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f4430/554, SYZ3 f6065/483. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309 and AIZ trace f8941/208 unchanged (S1-FZ-only change cannot affect them).

## 2026-06-24 - S1 SLZ Staircase executes from highest child slot (fan-before-ride-reseat, ExecuteObjects slot order); SLZ2 f2554 -> f2898 (156 -> 121)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-staircase-multipiece-model` off `origin/develop` 837ba1256 (worktree `agent-a74fd1ca7dce17e36`).
- Command: `mvn -o surefire:test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off" -Dmse=off`.
- First error before fix: f2554 `y` exp=0x0211 act=0x0212 (1px). The player rides the rising flipped staircase's piece3 (100% counter); both engine and ROM ride the SAME piece and the staircase counter matches ROM byte-for-byte (verified ctr 61->69 across f2545-2553). The 1px is the continued-ride EXIT timing: ROM exits the wide ride at the walk-off right edge one frame before the engine.
- Root (verified by per-object x-delta diag): the +2px walk-off push is the SLZ Fan (Obj5D `Sonic1FanObjectInstance.applyWind` -> `player.shiftX`, 5D Fan.asm:75). In ExecuteObjects (ascending SST slot) ROM runs the fan BEFORE the ridden staircase child block, so the fan pushes `centreX` 0x1809 -> 0x180B (relX 0x34 -> 0x36, off the exclusive `blo` right edge, sub SolidObject.asm:21-44) BEFORE the child's `Stair_Solid` continued-ride re-check -> ROM exits the ride, keeps the prior seat (y 0x0211). ROM slot order: `Stair_Main` allocates parent then 3 children via `FindNextFreeObj` which scans forward from the parent (sub FindFreeObj.asm:32-48), so children land ABOVE the parent; the fan loads from the layout into a slot between them (objpos/slz2.bin: staircase idx124, fan idx125, both X=0x1790). The engine folds parent+3 children into ONE instance at the PARENT's (low) slot, so the ride re-seat ran BEFORE the fan push -> engine saw pre-push 0x1809 (relX 0x34, still in $1B ride bounds), re-seated one frame too long (y 0x0212).
- Fix: `Sonic1StaircaseObjectInstance` reserves its 3 ROM child slots via `ObjectManager.allocateChildSlotsAfter` on its first update (FindNextFreeObj parity) and overrides `getExecutionSlotIndex()` to execute the consolidated instance from the highest reserved child slot (after the fan). Pattern mirrors `AizGiantRideVineObjectInstance.getExecutionSlotIndex`. The prior Y-direction fan/staircase slot-order fix (`getPlayerCentreYAtExecStart`) is KEPT (now harmless ‚ÄĒ the fan naturally precedes the staircase).
- After: SLZ2 f2554 -> f2898 (156 -> 121). New frontier f2898 `y_speed` exp=-0x0A80 act=0x0000: an unrelated launch/bounce divergence much later in the act, exposed by progressing 344 frames further.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 837ba1256, first-error frame + count): the staircase (Obj5B) is placed only in SLZ, so only SLZ traces can change. SLZ1 f2872/164 and SLZ3 f814/1024 BYTE-IDENTICAL. Non-SLZ verified byte-identical against develop: SYZ1 f4430/554, GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ3 f8499/1525, SBZ2 f1447/977 (plus LZ1 f5745/662, MZ2 f2819/1043, SBZ1 f3971/560, MZ3 f2079/1123). GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 GREEN. S3K AIZ f1095/4309 unchanged. Units: CollisionSystemTest, TestSolidRoutineProfiles, TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn, TestSonic1SpringObjectInstance, TestSonic1StaircaseActivation, TestSonic1StaircaseWallCollision, TestObjectManagerChildSlotAllocation, TestSlotAllocator, TestRewindCoverageGuard (87/0) PASS.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 Lava Geyser head defers first Geyser_Action one frame after spawn; MZ2 f2819 -> f2823 (1116 -> 1043), MZ1 cascade 205 -> 169

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-mz2-geyser` off `origin/develop` 861f59dd3 (worktree `.worktrees/slots`).
- Command: `mvn test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Mz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f2819 `g_speed` exp=0x0018 act=0x0000 (ROM Sonic grounded+running; engine HURT-bounced air=1, x_speed=0x200, y_speed=-0x400). Sonic position byte-perfect through f2819 (x=0x0395,y=0x044C both); the GeyserMaker (Obj0x4C) is in OST slot 0x3E in both engine and ROM (materialization slot matches) ‚ÄĒ so the spurious touch is purely the lava wall falling ~1 frame early onto Sonic.
- Root: ROM `Geyser_Index` is a `jmp` dispatch (docs/s1disasm/_incObj/"4C, 4D MZ Lava Geyser and Maker.asm":139): the Obj0x4D HEAD runs `Geyser_Main` (routine 0, init + addq.b #2,obRoutine, asm:157-167) on its spawn frame and RETURNS without running `Geyser_Action`; the gravity (addi.w #$18,obVelY) + SpeedToPos of `Geyser_Action` (routine 2, asm:235-242) run the NEXT frame. The engine's `update()` ran `ensureInitialized()` (Geyser_Main) and `updateHead()` (Geyser_Action) the same frame, so the lavafall column fell a frame early.
- Fix: `ranGeyserMainThisFrame` one-shot in `Sonic1LavaGeyserObjectInstance` ‚ÄĒ run Geyser_Main init on the spawn frame, then return, deferring the head's first Geyser_Action to the next frame. THIRD piece is constructed already-initialized at routine 2 (acts immediately, ROM-correct higher-slot same-pass); BODY (routine 4) tracks the head ‚ÄĒ both unaffected.
- After: MZ2 f2819 -> f2823 (1116 -> 1043). New frontier f2823 `x_speed` exp=0x0200 act=0x0000: the GeyserMaker's free-running eruption cadence materialises a few frames early (placement-materialization-frame issue, MTZ1-prime family) ‚ÄĒ NOT this object-local action-frame defer; deferred to the placement-spawn-frame determinism root. Bonus: MZ1 (f2101 frontier) cascade 205 -> 169 (same first-error frame).
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop 861f59dd3, first-error frame + count): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. All other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: MZ3 f2079/1123, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ3 f7952/1778, SLZ1 f2872/164, SLZ2 f2552/137, SLZ3 f814/1024, SBZ1 f3971/560. Units: `TestSonic1LavaGeyserOutOfRange` (4/0) PASS.
## 2026-06-24 - S1 curled Roller not collidable until it activates; SYZ1 f2338 -> f4430 (359 -> 554)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-syz1-f2338` off `origin/develop` 861f59dd3 (worktree `.worktrees/batbrain`).
- Command: `mvn test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Syz1CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f2338 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0200 (also `y_speed`/`g_speed`/rtn/rings). Engine state: rtn=04 (Sonic_Hurt), rings 0x1F->0, knocked up-left, LostRing objects spawned. ROM: rtn=02, rings stay 31, falling straight down unscathed. Player x/y matched ROM through f2337; the hurt fired AT f2338.
- Root: the engine Roller (Obj 0x43, slot 34 @0710,0253) was still in its initial waiting state (STATE_ROLL_CHK, never activated ‚ÄĒ verified by per-frame engine instrumentation: state=0/inv=false/col=0E the whole window; player fell pX 0742->070A, only ~50px right of the Roller, never the >256px the activation needs). ROM `Roll_Main` (routine 0) never sets `obColType`, and `Roll_Action_FromLeft` (ob2ndRout=0) leaves it untouched (43 Badnik - Roller.asm:19-38,86-100); `obColType` is first written on activation ($8E, :96) or stop-and-unfold ($0E, :177) ‚ÄĒ both advance ob2ndRout away from 0 and never return. So a dormant curled Roller has `obColType=0` (col_none) and ReactToItem skips it (`move.b obColType(a1),d0 / bne ...`, Sonic ReactToItem.asm:52-53). The engine's `getCollisionFlags()` returned 0x0E from spawn, so the dormant Roller was a live enemy and hurt the falling Sonic.
- Fix: `getCollisionFlags()` returns 0 while `secondaryState == STATE_ROLL_CHK` (mirrors ROM's unset obColType; the Roller never re-enters STATE_ROLL_CHK once activated, so this is equivalent and needs no new field ‚ÄĒ rewind-safe).
- After: SYZ1 f2338 -> f4430 (+2092-frame advance). New frontier f4430 `x_speed`/`y_speed`/`g_speed` on a RIGHTWALL slope (angle 0xA8); player x/y/angle match ROM, rings=0 in BOTH (Sonic legitimately lost rings later) ‚ÄĒ an unrelated slope physics root. Error count 359 -> 554 is the longer post-f4430 cascade now reached (frontier-frame is the metric).
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs develop baseline 861f59dd3, first-error frame + count): GHZ1/GHZ2 GREEN. Every other S1 complete-run BYTE-IDENTICAL: FZ f713/155, GHZ3 f6464/271, MZ1 f2101/169, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f7952/1778, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f3971/560, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SLZ1 f2872/164, SLZ2 f2552/137, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ3 f6065/483. Cross-game: S2 EHZ1 PASS, S3K AIZ f1095/4309 byte-identical. Units: `TestS1BadnikGenericRecreate` (12), `TestRewindCoverageGuard` (1) PASS.
## 2026-06-24 - S1 SLZ Fan reads pre-object-seat player Y (ExecuteObjects slot order); SLZ2 f2552 -> f2554 (137 -> 156)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-slz2-f2552-fanorder` off `origin/develop` 861f59dd3 (worktree `.worktrees/ordering`).
- Command: `mvn test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f2552 `x` exp=0x1806 act=0x1808 (+2px), `camera_x` +2. The earlier memory "fan-vs-wall / riser SolidObject negates the +2" hypothesis was DISPROVEN: there is no wall (ROM x_speed=0xE4, Sonic moving right freely, not blocked). Decisive ROM math: f2552 x=1806.1900 = f2551(1805.3500) + x_speed(0xE4), no +2 term; the fan FIRST pushes at f2553 (1808.F100 = f2552 + x_speed(0xD8) + 2px). So the engine pushes the fan ONE FRAME EARLY.
- Root: the fan's Y-range gate (`dy = playerY + $60 - fanY`, in-range `0<=dy<$70`) is dy=0 at f2552 in the engine (playerY=0210 -> push) but ROM's fan reads playerY=020F (dy=-1, out). The 1px Y diff is execution order: Sonic RIDES the SLZ staircase (Obj5B) which re-seats his Y to 0210 each frame as he climbs steps. In ROM the staircase child Sonic rides is a HIGHER slot than the fan (Obj5D), so it re-seats AFTER the fan reads Y; the fan sees the pre-step-lift Y. The engine folds the staircase into a LOWER slot than the fan, so the live Y already reflects the step lift -> fan reads it -> pushes a frame early. The fan force itself is byte-perfect +2 (recomputed by hand: facingRight, no-reverse, pX=1806/pY=0210 -> +2, matching the engine and the f2556 in-air +2). docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5D SLZ Fan.asm:53-56,75.
- Fix: `ObjectManager.captureExecStartPlayerCentreY` snapshots each player's centre Y at the start of the object exec pass (post-physics, pre-any-object re-seat); `getPlayerCentreYAtExecStart` exposes it; the fan reads it for the Y gate instead of the live centre Y. Object-local (only the fan reads the snapshot; the capture is inert for all other objects). Earlier `getCentreY(1)` (previous-frame) experiment fixed f2552 but regressed f307 (-4px, a DIFFERENT airborne fan scenario), proving the faithful value is the exec-start Y, not the previous frame's.
- After: SLZ2 f2552 -> f2554 (f2552/f2553 now byte-match ROM). New frontier f2554 `y` exp=0x0211 act=0x0212 (+1) is the PARKED SLZ staircase ride-seat +1px-Y family (engine st=28 still pushing vs ROM st=20), unrelated to the fan. SLZ2 error count rises 137 -> 156 as the trace now reaches that deeper pre-existing root two frames later (frontier-advance downstream cascade, not a regression of previously-passing content).
- Regression sweep (per-class surefire, with-fix vs known baselines): SLZ1 f2872/164, SLZ3 f814/1024, LZ1 f5745/662, SBZ1 f3971/560, SYZ1 f2338/359, MZ2 f2819/1116 BYTE-IDENTICAL; GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2 GREEN. Cross-game (shared ObjectManager touch): S2 EHZ1 PASS, S3K AIZ f1095/4309 byte-identical.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 leftward horizontal camera scroll uncapped (FixBugs=0); SYZ1 f816 -> f2338 (360 -> 359), MZ1 cascade 204 -> 169

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-syz1-floatingblock` off `origin/develop` 778efd727 (worktree `.worktrees/batbrain`).
- Command: `mvn test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Syz1CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f816 `camera_x` exp=0x0193 act=0x0194 (1px). Reclassified from the assigned "FloatingBlock riding miss": Sonic's x/x_speed match ROM every frame (the bounce off the LR spring @0218 to +0x1000 fires correctly in the engine). The 1px is pure horizontal-camera-scroll. At f816 Sonic rode a leftward FloatingBlock (x 0234->0223), so the camera needed to move -17px left in one frame (01A4 -> 0193); the engine capped it at -16 -> 0194.
- Root: S1 ScrollHoriz caps the RIGHTward camera move at 16 (SH_MoveCameraRight, unconditional) but the LEFTward cap (SH_MoveCameraLeft) is gated `if FixBugs`, and the shipped ROM has FixBugs=0 (docs/s1disasm/sonic.asm:20), so SH_MoveCameraLeft falls straight to .moveLeft and adds the full (possibly >16px) offset uncapped (docs/s1disasm/_inc/ScrollHoriz & ScrollVertical.asm:59-99). Cross-game: S2 caps both directions at 16 (s2.asm:18102-18105 .scrollLeft), S3K caps both at $18 (sonic3k.asm:38403-38406). So this is an S1-only divergence; the engine's shared `Camera.computeNextHorizontalCameraX` capped both.
- Fix: `PhysicsFeatureSet.uncappedLeftwardHorizontalScroll` (true for SONIC_1, false for SONIC_2/SONIC_3K), plumbed to `Camera.setUncappedLeftwardScroll` from `LevelManager` next to the existing fastScrollCap wiring; gates ONLY the leftward branch in `computeNextHorizontalCameraX`.
- After: SYZ1 f816 -> f2338 (360 -> 359 errors; ~1522-frame advance). New frontier f2338 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0200 is an unrelated physics root. Bonus: S1 MZ1 (f2101 frontier from the merged camera-boundary fix) cascade 204 -> 169 errors (same first-error frame) ‚ÄĒ MZ1 also has fast-leftward camera moments.
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs no-fix baseline on 778efd727, first-error frame + count): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN. All other S1 complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, LZ3 f7952/1778, GHZ3 f6464/271, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f3971/560, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SLZ1 f2872/164, SLZ2 f2552/137, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ3 f6065/483, FZ f713/155. Cross-game shared-Camera/PhysicsFeatureSet check: S2 EHZ1 PASS, S3K AIZ f1095/4309 byte-identical. Units: `TestPhysicsProfile`, `TestPhysicsProfileRegression`, `TestCamera`, `TestCameraRewindSnapshot`, `TestLookScrollDelay`, `TestCollisionModel` (97 tests) PASS.
## 2026-06-24 - S1 conveyor act-3 out_of_range left-extension; LZ3 f7952 -> f8499 (1778 -> 1525)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-conveyor-fidelity` (worktree `.worktrees/conveyor`), stacked on the LZ1 conveyor spawn-frame fix below.
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Lz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f7952 `y` exp=0x03C2 act=0x03CC (10px). Same class as LZ1 (Sonic walks off conveyor slot 0x4E onto 0x4B, engine misses the landing) but the LZ1 spawn-frame fix did NOT help. Diagnosed engine-side: the LZ3 path-4 spawner @0D00,0280 makes 12 children (all baseX=0x0D00); they init fine but ALL despawn when the camera scrolls right past the cluster. A frame-counter-label confound nearly misled me ‚ÄĒ the unload diag logged `camL=0E02` at object-frame "f7002", but the per-frame diag showed engine camera 0x0D3F at trace f7002; cross-referencing the trace, ROM camera_x=0x0E02 occurs at trace f7124 (Sonic moved right to 0x0EA2). So the unloads are at the real moment the camera is 0x0E02, where baseX 0x0D00 is one aligned chunk (0x80) behind the window's left edge -> out of range in BOTH ROM and engine. The consumed spawner's v_obj63 bit stays latched (ROM too), so the cluster never respawns; only 1 platform remained when Sonic backtracked left to f7952.
- Root: ROM act 3 keeps these platforms alive for one extra chunk to the left. `LabyrinthConvey`/`SpinConvey` out_of_range tail (`loc_1236A`/`SpinC_OutOfRange`) runs `cmpi.b #act3,(v_act).w` then `cmpi.w #-$80,d0 / bhs.s LCon_Display` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/63 LZ Conveyor.asm:16-20`; `6F SBZ Spin Platform Conveyor.asm:17-21`) ‚ÄĒ keep (don't delete, display) when d0 (unsigned) >= 0xFF80, i.e. aligned baseX within one chunk LEFT of the window. The engine's `isPersistent()` used the standard window for all acts.
- Fix: new shared `AbstractObjectInstance.isInRangeAtWithLeftExtension(referenceX, leftChunks)` (mirrors `bhs #-(leftChunks*$80)`); both conveyors call it with `leftChunks=1` when `services().currentAct()==ACT3` (act index 2), else the standard window. ROM-act branch, not a zone/trace carve-out.
- After: LZ3 f7952 -> f8499 (1778 -> 1525). New f8499 root is `g_speed` exp=0 act=-0x12 on an airborne bounce (stand=0x2A, x frozen 0x0C60) ~250px below any conveyor ‚ÄĒ unrelated.
- SBZ1 f3971 is NOT a conveyor root: the spin conveyors are ~250px above Sonic and there are ZERO objects within 56px of him at the divergence; ROM stops his airborne x_speed (0x0165->0) via a TERRAIN wall the engine misses. Out of scope for this task.
- Regression sweep (per-class surefire .txt, full S1): only LZ3 changed. GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 GREEN; LZ1 f5745/662, LZ2 f1068/1616, SBZ1 f3971/560, SBZ2 f1395/1000, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SLZ1 f2872/164, SLZ2 f2552/137, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f816/360, SYZ3 f6065/483, GHZ3 f6464/271 all byte-identical. Units + S3K keep-green 112/0.
## 2026-06-24 - S1 SBZ Saw unloads off-screen (saw_origX anchor) instead of staying persistent; SBZ2 f1395 -> f1447 (1000 -> 977)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-jaws-unload-phase` off `origin/develop` 778efd727 (worktree `.worktrees/rng`).
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Sbz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Ds1.rom.path=s1.gen" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"` (+ DebugS1Lz2BubblesOccupancyProbe trace.zone=5 trace.act=1).
- Board-root correction: the prior "SBZ2 = frame-0 boss-arena placement (slot32 0x6E vs 0x26)" was a wrong-indices artifact (probe run with the wrong zone/act). With SBZ2 = engine zone=5 act=1 the frame-0 slot table matches ROM exactly.
- Root (occupancy oracle): first object-table divergence f239 slot 0x61 ‚ÄĒ ROM Ring (0x25), engine SBZ Saw (0x6A). The engine RETAINED two off-screen Saws (`@0298,038B`/`@02A0,018B`, ~239px left of camera 0x387) that ROM unloaded, pushing the rings ROM placed in 0x61/0x62 down to 0x63/0x64. `Sonic1SawObjectInstance.isPersistent()` returned `!isDestroyed()` so the saw never unloaded; ROM `Saw_Action` ends with `out_of_range.s .delete, saw_origX(a0)` (`6A SBZ Saws and Pizza Cutters.asm:39`).
- Fix: `isPersistent()`->false, `getOutOfRangeReferenceX()`->origX (saw_origX; ground saws update it to the moved obX at lines 338/400, pizza cutters keep the origin), `checksOutOfRangeAfterRoutine()`->true (ROM checks post-move). Object-local to Obj 0x6A.
- After: SBZ2 f1395 -> f1447 (1000 -> 977); first object-table divergence f239 -> f350 (new root: ROM has Obj 0x78 animal/explosion in slot 0x63 the engine lacks). Regression sweep: SBZ1 f3971/560 byte-identical, GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2 GREEN, LZ2 f1068/1616, MZ2 f2819/1116, SLZ1 f2872/164 unchanged; CollisionSystemTest 54/0, TestSolidRoutineProfiles 13/0, TestRewindCoverageGuard 1/0, TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn 1/0.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 badnik out_of_range unload runs post-move (checksOutOfRangeAfterRoutine); LZ2 Jaws f196 unload fixed (object-table f196 -> f217; committed metric flat 1616/f1068)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-jaws-unload-phase` off `origin/develop` 778efd727 (worktree `.worktrees/rng`).
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Lz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Ds1.rom.path=s1.gen" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"` (+ DebugS1Lz2BubblesOccupancyProbe WATCH_SLOT=37).
- Premise correction (banked): the prior "level-init RNG-consumption-order" framing was disproved by a bounded GameRng caller audit (LZ2 maker consumes the raw seed, zero pre-maker draws; engine RNG order already matches ROM). The real first object-table divergence is RNG-free.
- Root (oracle: trace object_removed vs engine via WATCH_SLOT=37): ROM removes the slot-37 Jaws (Obj 0x2C `@012F,0430`, x_vel=-0x40) at object_removed f196; the engine kept it until f197 (one frame late). ROM Jaws_Swim = SpeedToPos then `bra.w RememberState`, and RememberState runs out_of_range on the CURRENT post-move x (`sub RememberState.asm:9`, `2C Badnik - Jaws.asm:58-59`). The engine's S1 counter-based exec loop checked out_of_range BEFORE execute (previous-frame position), so moving badniks unloaded one frame late, drifting slot occupancy.
- Fix: `ObjectInstance.checksOutOfRangeAfterRoutine()` (default false) overridden true on `AbstractBadnikInstance` (all S1 badniks are SpeedToPos->RememberState; per-object asm tails cited in the override). In `updateCounterBasedExecThenLoad`, when true SKIP the pre-execute check and re-check out_of_range AFTER executeObjectWithSolidContext; else keep pre-execute. Global order NOT flipped; mirrors runExecLoop (S2/S3K). Conveyor isPersistent-while-uninitialised workaround left intact (independent hook, no conflict).
- After: LZ2 first object-table divergence f196 -> f217 (slot-37 Jaws unloads f196 like ROM; engine slot37 empty f196-216 per WATCH_SLOT). Committed `obj_s20_slot` metric UNCHANGED at f1068/1616 ‚ÄĒ that symptom's root is the separate downstream f217 ring-sparkle(0x25 @00B8,00B8 ROM-spawned, engine omits)-vs-Jaws-respawn slot interleave (deep, deferred).
- Regression sweep (FULL S1 frame+count; S1-counter-path scoped, S2/S3K use runExecLoop): GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2 GREEN; GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/662, LZ3 f7952/1778, MZ1 f2101/204, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SLZ1 f2872/164, SLZ2 f2552/137, SLZ3 f814/1024, SYZ1 f816/360, SYZ3 f6065/483, SBZ1 f3971/560, SBZ2 f1395/1000, FZ f713/155 ‚ÄĒ all byte-identical baselines. S2 EHZ1 GREEN; S3K ICZ f3139/3179 unchanged. S3K keep-green AIZ 8/0, LevelLoading 5/0+30/0, Bootstrap 5/0, Decoding 3/0; CollisionSystemTest 54/0, TestSolidRoutineProfiles 13/0, TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn 1/0.

## 2026-06-24 - S1 conveyor platform survives its spawn frame before lazy baseX init; LZ1 f5745 camera_y -> 1px y (2214 -> 662)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-conveyor-fidelity` off `origin/develop` dae1bf67f (worktree `.worktrees/conveyor`).
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Lz1CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f5745 `camera_y` exp=0x028E act=0x028C (a 2px symptom). Diagnosed (engine-side, no BizHawk) with a temp per-frame dump of all Obj 63/6F instances + the trace's `object_near` ROM data: the LZ1 spawner `@1070,0280` (subtype 0x80, v_obj63 slot 0) fires once at f5281 and creates 8 child platforms (subtypes 0x00/0x02x3/0x04/0x05x3). The three subtype-0x02 children were despawned ON their spawn frame (f5281/f5282) with `baseX=0`, `init=false` ‚ÄĒ before `ensureInitialized()` ran. Because the consumed spawner's `v_obj63` bit stays latched, they never respawned, leaving a permanent gap in the conveyor loop.
- Root: the platform loads `baseX` (ROM `objoff_30`) lazily in `ensureInitialized()` on its first `update()`; until then `baseX=0`. The object's `isPersistent()` mirrors ROM `out_of_range` via `isInRangeAt(baseX)`, so an uninitialized platform reads as far off-screen and is unloaded by the exec loop's out-of-range pre-pass (`ObjectManager.updateCounterBasedExecThenLoad`, the line-714 `unloadCounterBasedOutOfRange` that runs BEFORE `executeObjectWithSolidContext`) on the spawn frame. ROM never hits this: `LabyrinthConvey` runs the routine (`LCon_Main`, which writes `objoff_30`) BEFORE the trailing `out_of_range.s loc_1236A,objoff_30(a0)` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/63 LZ Conveyor.asm:5-13,53-63`; SBZ `SpinC_Main` identical, `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/6F SBZ Spin Platform Conveyor.asm:5-13,37-54`). The missing platforms removed the one ROM carries Sonic on at f5745: ROM walks him off slot 0x20 (f5736) and lands him on slot 0x48 @1012,02EE (f5745); the engine had no platform there so he free-fell.
- Fix: `isPersistent()` returns `true` while `!initialized` in both `Sonic1LZConveyorObjectInstance` and `Sonic1SpinConveyorObjectInstance` (the platform initializes on its update this frame; the next frame's check uses the real baseX). Object-local to Obj 63 / Obj 6F.
- After: LZ1 2214 -> 662 errors; first error stays at f5745 but is now `y` exp=0x02EE act=0x02ED (1px) ‚ÄĒ Sonic lands on the platform, 1px low. Cross-checked vs trace `object_near` ROM data: at f5671 two of the three left-side ascending platforms match ROM byte-for-byte (ROMs96 @1022,02D9 == eng @1022,02D9; ROMs97 @1022,0262 == eng @1022,0262), the lead one is 1px/1frame ahead (ROMs72 @1022,0351 vs eng @1022,0350) ‚ÄĒ a residual sub-pixel corner-rounding phase on a single platform, a distinct deeper root.
- LZ3 (f7952/1778) and SBZ1 (f3971/560) UNCHANGED ‚ÄĒ their frontiers diverge before/independent of this despawn race.
