- Regression sweep (per-class surefire .txt): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN; every other S1 complete-run byte-identical (LZ2 f1068, LZ3 f7952, SBZ1 f3971, MZ1 f2089, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SLZ1 f164, SLZ2 f137, SLZ3 f1024, SYZ1 f360, SYZ3 f483, GHZ3 f6464). Units: `CollisionSystemTest`/`TestSolidRoutineProfiles`/`TestCollisionLogic`/`TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn` 69/0, `TestRewindCoverageGuard` (no new gap) + S3K `TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless`/`TestSonic3kLevelLoading` 44/0.
## 2026-06-24 - Camera bottom-boundary airborne +8 acceleration deferred one frame to the camera clamp; S1 MZ1 f2089 -> f2101 (205 -> 204)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-camera` off `origin/develop` dae1bf67f (worktree `.worktrees/batbrain`).
- Command: `mvn test -Dmse=off "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Mz1CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f2089 `camera_y` exp=0x02BE act=0x02C4 (6px). Player byte-identical to ROM (same sub-pixel, velocity); pure camera vertical-settle. Diagnosed by instrumenting `Camera.updateBoundaryEasing`: at f2089 (Sonic just airborne) the engine eased `maxY` 0x02BC -> 0x02C4 (+8, airborne acceleration) and then `updatePosition` clamped the boundary-pinned camera straight to 0x02C4 the SAME frame.
- Root: ROM `DeformLayers` runs `ScrollVertical` (camera move + clamp to the current `v_limitbtm2`) BEFORE `DynamicLevelEvents` (`docs/s1disasm/_inc/DeformLayers (REV01).asm:16-18`); `.move_boundary_down` accelerates the bottom boundary +2 -> +8/frame once Sonic is airborne and the camera is within 8px of it (`docs/s1disasm/_inc/DynamicLevelEvents.asm:35-49`). Since the boundary update runs AFTER the camera move, the +8 rate change is consumed by the NEXT frame's camera. The engine eases boundaries BEFORE moving the camera (`updateBoundaryEasing()` then `updatePosition()` in `LevelFrameStep`/`GameLoop`), so the +8 reached the camera one frame early. A global order swap (camera-move then ease) was tried and REJECTED ‚ÄĒ it regressed GHZ2 f3349 (the rising-boundary-under-roll case relies on the camera following the freshly-snapped boundary same-frame, ROM `SV_BottomBoundaryMoving`) and overshot MZ1 to f2019.
- Fix: a derived `cameraClampMaxY` that `updatePosition` clamps to instead of `maxY`. In the boundary-descending direction it advances by the step `maxY` took on the PREVIOUS frame (constant +2 keeps perfect pace; the +8 jump reaches the camera one frame late, matching ROM ScrollVertical reading the prior frame's `v_limitbtm2`). The ascending/snap path still uses `maxY` same-frame (GHZ2). `cameraClampMaxY` is derived: re-synced on `setMaxY` (immediate boundary writes) and reset to the converged value on rewind `restore`, so no `CameraSnapshot` field is needed.
- After: MZ1 f2089 -> f2101 (205 -> 204 errors). New frontier f2101 `camera_y` exp=0x02E8 act=0x02EA is a DEEPER root: with the camera-read timing fixed, the engine's underlying `maxY` boundary easing itself is ~6px ahead of ROM's `v_limitbtm2` during the +8->+2 deceleration tail (the camera becomes boundary-limited again and reads the diverged boundary). Precisely matching ROM's `v_limitbtm2` easing requires the recorder to capture `v_limitbtm2` per frame (not currently in the trace) ‚ÄĒ see follow-up. NOTE (from memory): MZ1 was previously characterized as non-greenable anyway (its 204-error count is dominated by a downstream ~f4230 physics cascade, not the camera).
- Regression sweep (with-fix vs no-fix baseline on dae1bf67f, first-error frame + count): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN. All other S1 camera-affected complete-runs BYTE-IDENTICAL: LZ1 f5745/2214, GHZ3 f6464/271, SLZ1 f2872/164, SYZ1 f816/360. Cross-game shared-Camera check: S2 EHZ1 PASS, S3K AIZ f1095/4309 byte-identical. Camera units: `TestCamera`, `TestCameraRewindSnapshot`, `TestLookScrollDelay` PASS (36 tests, only the still-red MZ1 frontier fails as expected).
- Follow-up: extend the recorder to capture S1 `v_limitbtm2` (+`f_bgscrollvert`) per frame so the engine `maxY` easing can be matched to ROM exactly (closes MZ1 f2101 and any other boundary-convergence-tail camera blips). SYZ1 f816 `camera_x` (this task's other target) is NOT a pure camera bug: ROM `onObj=0x28` (Sonic rides the FloatingBlock @0200,03C3) but engine `ride=0` ‚ÄĒ a riding/position symptom (like LZ1/GHZ1's camera_y), to be chased object-side.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 Seesaw tilt target computed post-player (atomic ChkSide->ChgFrame); SLZ3 f745 -> f814

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-slz3-f745` off `origin/develop` 55a2f5b16 (worktree `.worktrees/slz3-f718`).
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Slz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f745 `y` exp=0x02D8 act=0x02DB (3px); also f756 (re-seat blip) and f786 (`y_speed` -0738, spikeball launch). Root diagnosed with the v3.5 `object_near` `obj_frame` aux (this session's recorder extension): the ROM seesaw (Obj 5E, slot 70) flips its tilt mapping frame `obj_frame` 0x02->0x01 at f745 (player rocking within 8px of centre x=0x740 -> See_ChkSide sets flat), while the engine flipped at f747 -- 2 frames late.
- Root: the engine latched the tilt target (`See_ChkSide`) in `onSolidContact`, which fires during the player's solid pass (BEFORE the post-physics object update), while `See_ChgFrame` (obFrame advance) ran in `update()`. Since `update()` runs after `onSolidContact` within the frame, See_ChgFrame advanced obFrame toward the PREVIOUS frame's target -> the tilt flip lagged a frame. ROM `See_Slope2` (routine 4, `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5E SLZ Seesaw.asm:71-118`) runs `See_ChkSide` then falls straight into `See_ChgFrame`, both inside ExecuteObjects AFTER the player slot moved (atomic, post-move x). The trace had NO `x` divergence (engine and ROM player x identical every frame), so the only defect was WHEN the engine read the player x for the tilt decision.
- Fix: compute `calculateTargetAngle` (See_ChkSide) inside `update()` immediately before `updateMappingFrame` (See_ChgFrame), gated on the standing bit (still maintained by `onSolidContact`). S1 runs objects after player physics (`objectsExecuteAfterPlayerPhysics=true`), so `update()` observes Sonic's post-move x; this keeps the ROM ChkSide->ChgFrame order atomic and post-move. Object-local to Obj 5E.
- After: SLZ3 f745 -> f814. Resolved the f745/f756 tilt re-seat blips AND the f786 spikeball-launch cascade (all three gone from the error list). New frontier f814 `y_speed` exp=-0A80 act=0x0000 / `air` 1 vs 0 = a deeper spikeball launch divergence (ROM springs Sonic up ~10.5px, engine doesn't yet). Error count 916 -> 1024 (longer new cascade past a +69-frame-advanced frontier).
- Regression sweep: SLZ1 (f2872, 164 err) and SLZ2 (f1714, 215 err) BYTE-IDENTICAL; MZ3 (f2079, 1123 err) unchanged; GHZ1/GHZ2/SYZ2/SBZ3 stay GREEN. Object-local (seesaw only), so no shared-code cross-game sweep needed. Unit tests pass: `TestSeesawBallGraphRewind` 6/0, `TestS1SlzBossSpikeballGraphRewind` 5/0, `TestSonic1LargeGrassyPlatformObjectInstance` 3/0, `TestCollisionLogic` 1/0.
## 2026-06-23 - S1 Vanishing Platform v_framecount gate + PlatformObject landing family; SBZ1 f2268 -> f3971 (805 -> 560)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-sbz1-f2268` in worktree `.worktrees/slz2-f1493`, off `origin/develop` 8977091a7. Two commits: v3.5 trace regen (a5ce4a9c4) + the fix.
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=TestS1Sbz1CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- **`s1_sbz1` advanced f2268 -> f3971, errors 805 -> 560.** First error before: f2268 `y_speed` exp=0x0000 act=0x0128 (ROM lands Sonic on the re-appeared vanishing platform `@0BB0,0648`; engine fell through). After: f3971 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=0x0172 ‚ÄĒ a distinct downstream root.
- Root 1 (cycle phase): ROM `VanP_Sync` reads `v_framecount` for the routine 6->2 gate (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/6C SBZ Vanishing Platforms.asm:51-56`); the engine `updateIdle` used the `update()` `frameCounter` param = VBla clock. Per-frame instrumentation: the target platform transitioned to routine 2 at vfc 2170 (VBla %256) instead of ROM's vfc 2048 (v_framecount %256), 122 frames out of phase -> vanished while ROM solid at f2268. Fix: gate on `levelManager.getFrameCounter()+1`. The **v3.5 trace's `object_near` `obj_frame`** confirmed the target platform is solid (obj_frame 0) at f2268 in both ROM and the engine after this fix (the diagnostic the regen was for).
- Root 2 (landing geometry): with the platform solid, the engine still didn't catch the falling player (engine y=0x062F airborne vs ROM y=0x062C landed). ROM `VanP` lands the first contact via `PlatformObject` and rides via `MvSonicOnPtfm2` (`6C ...asm:82-93`) ‚ÄĒ the Obj 18 top-solid family. The platform was missing those overrides. Fix: `HALF_HEIGHT` 8->9 (MvSonicOnPtfm2 `subi #9`), `usesCollisionHalfWidthForTopLanding()`, `rejectsZeroDistanceTopSolidLanding()`, `getTopLandingSnapAdjustment()=-1` (PlatformObject obY-8 detect). The counter fix alone regressed 805->836 (solid at the right time but the missing landing family meant no catch); the two together land Sonic and advance the frontier.
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SBZ3 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. All sibling S1 frontiers byte-identical: SBZ2 f1395, MZ2 f2819, SLZ1 f2872, LZ1 f5745. Object-local to Obj 6C (SBZ-only). Unit: `CollisionSystemTest` 54/0, `TestSolidRoutineProfiles` 13/0, `TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn` 1/0.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 MZ3 complete-run regenerated at recorder v3.4 for object_near aux (frontier UNCHANGED f2079 / 1123 errors)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-mz3-f2079` off `origin/develop` 48108c98e (worktree `.worktrees/slz3-f718`).
- The committed MZ3 trace was a stale recorder v3.2 recording with NO `aux_state.jsonl` (only metadata.json + physics.csv.gz). Regenerated with `tools/bizhawk/s1_complete_run_recorder.lua` v3.4 (`S1_STOP_AT_FRAME=65000`, s1.gen, fast-headless) so the trace now carries per-frame `object_near` aux (ROM per-object x/y/routine/status near the player). This is DIAGNOSTIC context only ‚ÄĒ `compareObjectNearEvents()` stays default-false, so object_near is rendered in the report context window but does NOT define new divergences (comparison-only invariant preserved). NO engine change in this commit.
- Frontier VERIFIED UNCHANGED: first divergence still f2079 `y_speed` exp=-02C8 act=-03C8, total 1123 errors ‚ÄĒ identical to the v3.2 trace (same BK2/ROM -> same physics; the regen only adds aux). The new metadata required a manual `source_bk2: "s1-complete-run.bk2"` field (the v3.4 recorder does not emit it; the shared `_movies/` BK2 resolver needs it, matching the already-regenerated lz2_completerun trace) or the test silently SKIPS.
- New diagnostic value (impossible without this regen): the ROM Batbrain (Obj 0x55, slot 74) trajectory now confirms the f2079 root quantitatively. ROM Batbrain hangs at y=0x048C (f2029-2053), drops ~1px/frame f2054-2062 to y=0x0494, then LOCKS flight-Y at 0x0494 and flies right; it is destroyed (slot 74 -> ObjID 0x27 explosion + 0x28 points) at f2079 by Sonic's bounce. The engine Batbrain locks flight-Y at 0x0491 -- **3px too high** (not 1px as first estimated) -- so its bottom edge (0x0499) misses the player's top edge (0x049A) and the React_BadnikHit +0x100 slowFromBelow bounce never fires. Engine drop stops 3px short of ROM (locks after ~5px descent vs ROM's 8px). Next: diagnose the engine Batbrain drop-stop (`Sonic1BatbrainBadnikInstance` distToTarget<0x10 / captured targetY / drop start) against this ROM trajectory.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 Seesaw used a non-zero slope baseline so SlopeObject landing fired 1 frame late; SLZ3 f718 -> f745 (1073 -> 916)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-slz3-f718` off `origin/develop` 0b0fb8ac7 (worktree `.worktrees/slz3-f718`).
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=TestS1Slz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before fix: f718 `y_speed` exp=0x0000 act=0x0610. ROM: rolling Sonic falling at y_speed=0x05D8 (f717) lands on the SLZ Seesaw (Obj 5E) at f718 ‚ÄĒ `y_speed` snaps to 0, `air` 1->0, `status` bit 3 set (on object), `g_speed = x_speed = -0168`. Engine kept falling (`y_speed=0x0610`, airborne) and only landed at f719 (one frame late).
- Root: `Sonic1SeesawObjectInstance.getSlopeBaseline()` returned `COLLISION_HEIGHT` (8). ROM `See_Slope` (routine 2, `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5E SLZ Seesaw.asm:67`) lands the player via `SlopeObject`, which uses ABSOLUTE slope values: `d0 = obY(a0) - heightmapByte` with no baseline subtraction (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub PlatformObject.asm:150-152`, then `Plat_NoXCheck_AltY`). The engine's `resolveSlopedContact` computes `baseY = anchorY - (slopeSample - slopeBaseline)`, so a baseline of 8 placed the sampled top surface 8px LOWER (larger Y) than ROM's `obY - slopeSample`. The falling player therefore had to drop ~8px further before the engine registered the top-solid landing, delaying it by one frame at the trace's fall speed.
- Fix: `getSlopeBaseline()` returns 0, citing the ROM absolute-slope semantics and matching the sibling `SlopeObject` user `Sonic1CollapsingLedgeObjectInstance` (which already returns 0 for the same reason). Object-local to Obj 5E (SLZ-only). The riding re-seat path (`sampleSlopeY`) is already baseline-independent, so continued-ride Y is unchanged.
- After: SLZ3 f718 -> f745, 1073 -> 916 errors. New frontier f745 `y` exp=0x02D8 act=0x02DB (3px): a single-frame seesaw-tilt re-seat phase blip (f743/f744 match, f745 ROM re-seats the rider up 3px one frame before the engine, both agree again at f746; same blip recurs at f756). The next substantial divergence is f786 (`y_speed`/`air`, a deeper player-physics root). Both new roots are NOT this baseline bug.
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, A/B by temporarily reverting the single-line edit): SLZ1 f2872 (164 errors) and SLZ2 f1714 (215 errors) byte-identical with and without the fix; GHZ1, GHZ2, SYZ2 stay GREEN. Unit tests pass: `TestSonic1LargeGrassyPlatformObjectInstance` 3/0, `TestSeesawBallGraphRewind` 6/0, `TestS1SlzBossSpikeballGraphRewind` 5/0, `TestCollisionLogic` 1/0.
## 2026-06-23 - S1 LR spring control lock decremented in air; SLZ2 f1714 -> f2552 (215 -> 137)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-slz2-f1493` in worktree `.worktrees/slz2-f1493`, detached at `origin/develop` 0b0fb8ac7.
- Command: `mvn test "-Dtest=TestS1Slz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- **`s1_slz2` advanced f1714 -> f2552, errors 215 -> 137.** First error before fix: f1714 `x` exp=0x15F2 act=0x15F3 (engine 1px right; `x_speed`/`g_speed` exp=-0F94 act=-0F14, +0x80; `x_sub` ROM C500 vs engine 4500, +0x8000). After: f2552 `x` exp=0x1806 act=0x1808 ‚ÄĒ a distinct StaircaseObject (0x5B) ride-position root (`onObj`/`onSlot` 0x5B, `ride=1`).
- Root: the S1 LR spring (Obj 41) bounced Sonic leftward off a ledge at f06A2 with `locktime=15`. ROM `Spring_BounceLR` writes `move.w #15,locktime(a1)` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/41 Springs.asm:145`); `locktime` (objoff_3E) is the same field S2's spring writes as `move_lock` (`docs/s2disasm/s2.asm:34031`). ROM only decrements `locktime` in `Sonic_SlopeRepel` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/01 Sonic.asm:1383,1410`), called by the grounded modes `Sonic_MdNormal`/`Sonic_MdRoll` but NOT the airborne `Sonic_MdJump`/`MdJump2` ‚ÄĒ so during the 6 airborne frames (f06AA-f06AF) the lock is frozen. At f06B2 ROM still had the lock active and ignored the just-pressed Right input (inertia held at F06C); the engine modeled the lock with `springingFrames`, which `tickStatus()` decrements every frame including airborne, so the engine's lock had expired and it applied the Right-deceleration (F06C->F0EC). Verified frame-by-frame via a temp `OGGF_DBG_SLZ2` dump in the trace loop (engine matched ROM through f06B1, diverged at f06B2; with the fix `mlock` froze at 7 across the airborne frames and f06B2 held F06C / xsub C500 = ROM).
- Fix: `Sonic1SpringObjectInstance.applyHorizontalSpring` now also calls `player.setMoveLockTimer(15)` (engine model of `locktime`, decremented only on grounded frames in `doSlopeRepel()`). `springing` is retained for the air-spring animation / carry marker consumed elsewhere. Object-local to Obj 41's horizontal path; only `Spring_LR` sets `locktime` (vertical/down springs and S2/S3K springs are untouched).
- Regression sweep (full S1 complete-run sweep, vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SBZ3 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. SLZ1 f2872/164 byte-identical. All other S1 frontiers unchanged: FZ f713/155, GHZ3 f6464/271, LZ1 f5745/2214, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f7952/1778, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f2268/805, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SLZ3 f718/1073, SYZ3 f6065/483. SYZ1 frontier frame/field UNCHANGED (f816 `camera_x`) but downstream-cascade error count 351 -> 360 (+9) in an already-red trace (SYZ1 hits LR springs; the post-spring lock window shifts a few later cascade frames ‚ÄĒ no new earlier divergence). Unit tests: `TestSonic1SpringObjectInstance` 2/0, `TestSpringObjectInstance` 10/0. S3K must-keep-green: `TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless` 8/0, `TestSonic3kLevelLoading` 30/0.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 type-03 platform fall timer missed the landing frame; GHZ1 f3246 -> GREEN (255 -> 0)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-ghz1-advance` off `origin/develop` 88a4be974; cherry-picked Orbinaut CalcSine fix (f9fac6f25) first to restore SLZ2 to its correct develop baseline before the platform fix.
- **`s1_ghz1` GREENED (255 -> 0 errors).** First error before fix: f3246 `y` exp=0x0171 act=0x0170 / `camera_y` exp=0x0111 act=0x0110 (1px; Sonic 1px too high on a type-03 falling platform). After: complete-run passes with 0 errors.
- Root: ROM `Plat_Solid` (routine 2) calls `PlatformObject` ‚ÄĒ which sets `obStatus` bit 3 (standing) AND changes routine to 4 ‚ÄĒ then falls through to `Plat_Action` which calls `Plat_Move`. `.type03` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/18 Platforms.asm:201-216`): `tst.w objoff_3A; bne .type03_wait; btst #3,obStatus; beq .nomove; move.w #30,objoff_3A`. So on the landing frame, the timer is set to 30. The engine's `moveFallOnStand()` runs BEFORE `checkpointAll()` (which is the engine's `PlatformObject` equivalent), so on the landing frame it read the PREVIOUS frame's `playerStanding=false`, missed the timer start, and started the 30-frame countdown 1 frame late. This meant the platform transitioned to type04 (falling, 16.16 gravity 0x38) 1 frame late, holding Sonic on the surface 1 frame longer. At GHZ1 f3246, the platform `@13A0,0188 subtype=03`: with correct timing the platform had already fallen 1px (type04 workingY advanced), setting its Y to 0x018D vs the engine's 0x018C; Sonic rode via `MvSonicOnPtfm2` (`platform.Y - 9 - 19`) = 0x0171 (ROM) vs engine 0x0170.
- Fix: after `checkpointAll()` sets `playerStanding` in `Sonic1PlatformObjectInstance.update`, added a post-checkpoint guard: `if (moveType == 0x03 && timer == 0 && playerStanding) { timer = FALL_STAND_DELAY; }`. This mirrors `.type03`'s first-time-standing detection on the landing frame. Object-local to Obj 18.
- Regression sweep (full S1 sweep, vs develop baseline including cherry-picked Orbinaut fix): GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN, all other S1 frontiers unchanged: GHZ3 f2693, LZ1 f5745, LZ2 f1068, LZ3 f6517, MZ1 f2089, MZ2 f2819, MZ3 f2079, SBZ1 f2268, SBZ2 f1395, SLZ1 f933, SLZ2 f1493, SLZ3 f718, SYZ1 f816, SYZ3 f6065. Unit tests: TestSolidRoutineProfiles 13/0, TestSonic1SpringObjectInstance 2/0, TestOrbinautBadnikInstance 6/0 ‚ÄĒ all pass.
- Note: this fix was developed in worktree `ghz1-advance` which was based on `origin/develop` 88a4be974 (before `f9fac6f25` Orbinaut fix). Without cherry-picking the Orbinaut fix first, the timer fix caused SLZ2 to regress from f1493 to f1016: the earlier platform fall timing changed Sonic's trajectory so he hit the Orbinaut spike 4 frames early (a pre-existing spike-orbit 1px Y offset that the Orbinaut fix addresses). Cherry-picking the Orbinaut fix into the branch first restored the correct behavior.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 SmashableWall setCentreX cleared sub-pixel on smash; GHZ3 f2693 -> f4650 (477 -> 363)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-ghz3-advance` off `origin/develop` (HEAD 8e3aefaaf).
- **`s1_ghz3` frontier advanced f2693 -> f4650 (477 -> 363 errors).** First error before fix: f2693 `x` exp=0x083C act=0x083B (1px too left). After: f4650 `x_speed` mismatch (next root, unrelated to this fix).
- Root: `Sonic1BreakableWallObjectInstance.smashWall` (lines 227/231 before fix) called `player.setCentreX((short) value)` to apply the ¬Ī4px x adjustment after smashing. ROM's equivalent (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/3C GHZ, SLZ Smashable Wall.asm` lines 57-64) uses `addq.w #4,obX(a1)` and `subq.w #8,obX(a1)` which modify ONLY the integer pixel word at `obX`, leaving `obSubX` (the sub-pixel word) intact. `setCentreX` clears the sub-pixel to 0. At f2691 (the smash frame), Sonic's sub-pixel was 0x1200 after the SolidObject snap (preserved correctly by the engine's `player.move` snap path). After the ROM-path `addq.w #4` the sub stays 0x1200, but the engine's `setCentreX` zeroed it to 0x0000. The accumulated sub-pixel difference meant that at f2693, Sonic's carry bit didn't fire (engine sub 0xF200+0x7600=no-carry, vs ROM sub 0x8E00+0x7600=carry), leaving the integer x 1px short.
- Fix: replaced `player.setCentreX(...)` with `player.shiftX(¬Ī4)` ‚ÄĒ `AbstractSprite.shiftX` adds to the integer pixel word only, sub-pixel preserved, exactly matching ROM `addq.w #4,obX(a1)` / `subq.w #8,obX(a1)`. Object-local to Obj 0x3C.
- Regression sweep (S1 broad sweep, vs develop baseline): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. SLZ2 f1493/277 (unchanged). All other S1 frontiers: LZ1 f5745, LZ2 f1068, LZ3 f6517, MZ1 f2089, MZ2 f2819, MZ3 f2079, SBZ1 f2268, SBZ2 f1395, SLZ1 f933, SLZ2 f1493, SLZ3 f718, SYZ1 f816, SYZ3 f6065 ‚ÄĒ all byte-identical. Unit tests: `TestSonic1BreakableWallObjectInstance` 2/0 pass.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 SpikedPoleHelix v_ani0_frame unseeded; GHZ3 f4650 -> f5043 (363 -> 6)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-ghz3-f4650` off `origin/develop` (HEAD c1a59c238).
- **`s1_ghz3` frontier advanced f4650 -> f5043 (363 -> 6 errors).** First error before fix: f4650 `x_speed` exp=0x0194 act=-0x0200 (sign flip; engine in HURT state, ROM grounded and running right). After: f5043 `x_speed` mismatch (next root).
- Root: `Sonic1SpikedPoleHelixObjectInstance` maintained per-object `animCounter=0` / `animTimer=11` fields initialized at construction (object spawn), ignoring the ROM's global `v_ani0_frame` counter which has been ticking since level start. At GHZ3 f4650 (gfc=0x1220), ROM `v_ani0_frame` = `(-ceil(0x1220/12)) & 7 = (-387) & 7 = 5`, so that particular spike was harmless (frame ‚Č† 0). The engine's `animCounter` had diverged from this phase, placing the spike at frame 0 (harmful), triggering a HURT on Sonic.
- Root type: identical to SBZ Electrocuter (commit `SBZ1 f1925`) ‚ÄĒ object reads a ROM global counter via per-object unseeded state instead of the trace-seeded level frame counter.
- Root (ROM): `Hel_RotateSpikes` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/17 GHZ Spiked Pole Helix.asm:95-105`): `move.b (v_ani0_frame).w,d0` ‚ÄĒ reads the GLOBAL `v_ani0_frame` (RAM 0xFEC1). `SynchroAnimate` (`docs/s1disasm/sonic.asm:3111-3119`) ticks `v_ani0_frame` every 12 `v_framecount` (gfc) ticks, starting with an underflow tick at gfc=1 (initial `v_ani0_time=0`). After gfc=N: `v_ani0_frame = (-ceil(N/12)) & 7` where `ceil(N/12) = (N+11)/12` (integer division).
- Fix: replaced the per-object `animCounter` accumulation in `update()` with a direct derivation from the trace-seeded level frame counter: `gfc = levelManager.getFrameCounter()+1; animCounter = (-((gfc + 11) / 12)) & 7`. This is computed every frame, matching ROM's read of the shared `v_ani0_frame`. Object-local to Obj 0x17 (GHZ-only).
- Regression sweep (full S1 broad sweep, vs develop baseline c1a59c238): GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. All other S1 frontiers byte-identical: LZ1 f5745, LZ2 f1068, LZ3 f6517, MZ1 f2089, MZ2 f2819, MZ3 f2079, SBZ1 f2268, SBZ2 f1395, SLZ1 f933, SLZ2 f1493, SLZ3 f718, SYZ1 f816, SYZ3 f6065. No unit test for helix (none existed).
## 2026-06-23 - S1 Staircase timer no-decrement on set frame + SolidObject airHalfHeight for fresh contacts; SLZ1 f933 -> f2872 (246 -> 164)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-s1-slz1-staircase` off `develop` 85edc9384.
- **`s1_slz1` advanced f933 -> f2872, errors 246 -> 164.** First error before: f933 `y` 1px (Sonic seated 1px too low on Obj 0x5B Staircase). After: f2872 `y` 1px (cascade ‚ÄĒ ROM Sonic on a CirclingPlatform, engine Sonic still on the staircase; caused by pre-staircase ring-count and x-position cascades).
- Root 1 (f933 timer): `Sonic1StaircaseObjectInstance.updateType00` ran the timer decrement on the same frame the timer was set. ROM `Stair_Type00` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5B SLZ Staircase.asm:104-119`): `tst.w timer; bne.s loc_10FC0; cmpi.b #1,playerTop; bne.s locret_10FBE; move.w #$1E,timer` then falls through to `locret_10FBE rts` ‚ÄĒ the decrement at `loc_10FC0` is only entered when the timer was already non-zero. Engine collapsed set+decrement into one frame, advancing the countdown by 1 each trigger. Fixed by `return` after `timer = TOP_CONTACT_DELAY` in both `updateType00` and `updateType02`. Same pattern in `Stair_Type02` at asm:122-137 (`move.w #$3C, timer` + fall-through to `locret_10FDE`).
- Root 2 (f2526‚Äďf2529 sibling snap): `processMultiPieceCollision` runs all non-riding pieces of the staircase with `resolveContact(apply=true)`. Non-riding sibling pieces at overlapping X ranges were applying `Solid_Landed` Y snaps that overrode the ridden-piece re-seat. In ROM, each piece occupies a separate SST slot; the ridden piece (higher slot) runs LAST and its `MvSonicOnPtfm`/`SolidObject` result is authoritative. Fix: track `ridingCentreYToRestore` from `processInlineRidingObject`, gate `putRidingState` on `ridingCentreYToRestore == MIN_VALUE`, and restore after the sibling pass.
- Root 3 (f2526‚Äďf2872 1px grounded snap): `processMultiPieceCollision` used `groundHalfHeight=17` for grounded players. ROM `SolidObject_cont` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub SolidObject.asm:170-176`; S2 equivalent `s2.asm:35361-35373`) always uses d2 (airHalfHeight=16) for the Y bounding-box window: `add.w y_radius, d2` where d2 is the input top half-height. Using `groundHalfHeight` inflated `maxTop` by 1, giving `distY=4` (engine) vs `distY=3` (ROM) and snapping Sonic 1px too low (`newCentreY = playerCentreY - 4 + 3 = -1` vs `-3 + 3 = 0`). Fix: unconditionally use `params.airHalfHeight()` in `processMultiPieceCollision` (correct for S1/S2/S3K ‚ÄĒ all three ROM `SolidObject` implementations use the top half-height in d2 for Y detection; `groundHalfHeight` is only used by `MvSonicOnPtfm` for the continued-ride re-seat).
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. All other S1 frontiers byte-identical: FZ f713/155, GHZ1 f3246/255, GHZ3 f2693/477, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2819/1116, MZ3 f2079/1123, SBZ1 f2268/805, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SLZ2 f1714/215, SLZ3 f718/1073, SYZ1 f816/351, SYZ3 f6065/483. 66 pre-existing unit test failures unaffected (rewind torture, MhzMushroomCap, TestNoServicesInObjectConstructors, TestTouchResponseManager, TestPlayableSpriteMovement ‚ÄĒ all present on develop baseline). Staircase unit tests: TestSonic1StaircaseActivation and TestSonic1StaircaseWallCollision pass.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 SpikedPoleHelix animCounter off-by-one + hurt-direction source X; GHZ3 f5043 -> f6464 (6 -> 271)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-ghz3-f5043` off `origin/develop`.
- **`s1_ghz3` frontier advanced f5043 -> f6464 (6 -> 271 errors).** First error before fix: f5043 `y_speed` exp=0 act=-0x0400 (HURT from @1688 helix spike at 0x16C8). After: f6464 `y` mismatch (different root).
- Root 1 ‚ÄĒ animCounter off-by-one: ROM `Level_MainLoop` runs `ExecuteObjects` at sonic.asm:2988 BEFORE `SynchroAnimate` at 3010. Objects at loop iteration gfc=N therefore read `v_ani0_frame` set by the PREVIOUS iteration's SynchroAnimate (after N-1 calls). The prior formula computed `ceil(N/12) = (N+11)/12` ticks (value after N calls), which is 1 tick too many when N is a multiple of 12. Correct formula: `(-((gfc+10)/12)) & 7` = value after (N-1) calls. At trace f5042 (gfc=5029), the old formula gave animCounter=4 making the phase=4 spike (@16C8) harmful; the correct value is 5 (phase=4 ‚Üí (5+4)&7=1, harmless). The spurious hurt fired 1 trace-frame early.
- Root 2 ‚ÄĒ hurt-direction source X: ROM `HurtSonic.checkDirection` (docs/s1disasm/_incObj/Sonic ReactToItem.asm:402-405) compares Sonic's `obX` against `obX(a2)` where `a2` = the individual child spike's RAM slot (x=0x16C8). Engine `applyHurt` used `instance.getX()` (parent helix x=0x1688). Sonic (x=0x16C6) > parent (0x1688), so engine negated ‚Üí rightward bounce (+0x200). Correct: Sonic (0x16C6) < spike (0x16C8), so no negate ‚Üí leftward (-0x200). Fix: added `regionX` field to `TouchResponseResult`; `processMultiRegionTouch` populates it from `region.x()`; `applyHurt` prefers `result.regionX()` when present.
- Regression sweep: GHZ1 GREEN, GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. 36 unit tests pass.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 Orbinaut satellite orbit used float Math.round instead of ROM CalcSine integer shift; SLZ2 f1016 -> f1493

- Branch: `bugfix/ai-s1-syz-slz-advance` off `develop` 326de21ad.
- **`s1_slz2` advanced f1016 -> f1493, errors 221 -> 277 (different cascade, higher total but later first-error).** First error before: f1016 `g_speed` exp=0x0295 act=0x0000 (hurt bounce; engine took early HURT from Orbinaut spike). After: f1493 `x` 1px on slope (a separate downstream frontier).
- Root: `OrbSpikeObjectInstance.update` computed orbital position with `Math.round(Math.cos(radians) * 16.0)`. ROM `Orb_CircleSpikeball` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/60 Badnik - Orbinaut.asm:181-191`) calls `jsr CalcSine` (d0=sine, d1=cosine; range -0x100..0x100) then `asr.w #4,d1` / `add.w obX(a1),d1` for X, same for sine/Y. The arithmetic right shift truncates: sine=0xFE=254, `254 >> 4 = 15`; floating-point: `Math.round(254.0/256.0 * 16.0) = Math.round(15.875) = 16`. At SLZ2 f1016, spike Y was ROM 0x00B7 vs engine 0x00B8 (1px low). Player touch-box top at 0x00BC; spike bottom ROM 0x00B7+4=0x00BB (misses), engine 0x00B8+4=0x00BC (boundary hit ‚Üí HURT fires 4 frames early). In ROM, Sonic wasn't hurt until f1020 after moving into the spike.
- Fix: replace float trig with `TrigLookupTable.sinHex(angle) >> 4` and `TrigLookupTable.cosHex(angle) >> 4` in `OrbSpikeObjectInstance.update` (`Sonic1OrbinautBadnikInstance.java`). Added `TrigLookupTable` import.
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): SYZ2 stays GREEN; GHZ2 stays GREEN. All LZ/SBZ frontiers unchanged: LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, SBZ1 f2268/805, SBZ2 f1395/1000. Unit tests: `TestSonic1CaterkillerBodyChaining` 6/0, `CollisionSystemTest` 54/0, `TestSolidRoutineProfiles` 13/0, `TestSonic1SpringObjectInstance` 2/0, `TestOrbinautBadnikInstance` (S3K) 6/0.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 platform walk-off frame missing the MvSonicOnPtfm2 Y re-seat; SYZ3 f3476 -> f6065 (485 -> 483), SYZ2 -> GREEN

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-syz3-advance` off `develop` d8589639d.
- **`s1_syz3` advanced f3476 -> f6065, errors 485 -> 483.** First error before: f3476 `y` exp=0x02C1 act=0x02C0 (1px; `camera_y` co-symptom). After: f6065 `x_speed` (a separate downstream frontier). **Bonus: `s1_syz2` f6845/55 -> GREEN** (the same exit-frame fidelity closed it).
- Root (BizHawk + ROM physics.csv + engine debug, s1.gen ROM arg): Sonic walks off a vertically-bobbing Obj18 platform (slot 0x42 @0CBA,02DD; `Plat_Nudge` makes obY oscillate via `objoff_2C + sinHex(objoff_38)*$400`). ROM Obj18 routine 4 (`Plat_Action2`) runs `ExitPlatform` (clears the on-object bit) and then unconditionally `MvSonicOnPtfm2` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/18 Platforms.asm:74-87`), which re-seats the rider's Y AND carries X: `obY(rider) = platform_Y - 9 - obHeight` (`sub MvSonicOnPtfm.asm:18-41`). On the walk-off frame the platform stepped 02DC->02DD, so ROM re-seated the rider to centre 02C1; the engine's `ObjectSolidContactController` exit block did the X carry but skipped the Y re-seat, leaving the rider at the pre-move surface centre 02C0. Verified frame-aligned (engine `getCentreY()` vs the platform x): at platX=cba/platY=2dd the engine centre held 2c0 while ROM was 02C1.
- Fix: in the `carriesAirborneRiderAfterExitPlatform && carriesRiderOnHorizontalMove` exit block, after the X carry, add the flat `MvSonicOnPtfm2` Y re-seat (`exitCentreY = currentY + offsetY - groundHalfHeight - yRadius; exitNewY = exitCentreY - height/2; player.setY(exitNewY)`), guarded `!(sampleSlopeOnRideExit && SlopedSolidProvider)` so the existing sloped-exit re-seat still owns the slope case. Shared `ObjectSolidContactController` change but GATED by `carriesAirborneRiderAfterExitPlatform` -> only Obj18 Platform, Obj52 MovingBlock, Obj59 Elevator (all three ROM equivalents call `MvSonicOnPtfm2` unconditionally on exit: `52 Moving Blocks.asm:83`, `59 SLZ Elevators.asm:93`).
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): all other S1 complete-runs byte-identical (GHZ1 f3246/255, GHZ3 f2693/477, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2578/1010, MZ3 f2079/1048, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, SLZ1 f933/246, SLZ2 f1016/221, SLZ3 f718/1073, SBZ1 f2268/805, SBZ2 f1395/1000, FZ f713/155, SYZ1 f816/351). GHZ2 + SBZ3 GREEN; SYZ2 now GREEN. S2 EHZ1 green; S3K AIZ f1095/4309 identical. Units: CollisionSystemTest 54/0, TestSolidRoutineProfiles 13/0, TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn 1/0, TestSonic1LargeGrassyPlatformObjectInstance 3/0, TestS1SwingingPlatformSurfaceRegression 1/0, TestSonic1MovingBlockObjectInstance 2/0, TestSonic1ElevatorObjectInstance 2/0, TestS1JumpFromElevator 1/0.

## 2026-06-23 - S1 LZConveyor platform PlatformObject contact uses pre-move position; LZ3 f6517 -> f7952 (1726 -> 1778)

- Branch/worktree: `bugfix/ai-s1-lz3-f6517` off `origin/develop` (HEAD c1a59c238-equivalent baseline).
- **`s1_lz3` frontier advanced f6517 -> f7952 (1726 -> 1778 errors).** First error before fix: f6517 `y_speed` exp=0x0000 act=0x0860 (Sonic airborne, ROM grounded on LZConveyor slot 0x4E). After: f7952 `y` exp=0x03C2 act=0x03CC (different root ‚ÄĒ a separate LZ Conveyor group or platform-family issue; engine `onSlot=47` vs ROM `stand_on_obj=0x4B`).
- Root: `Sonic1LZConveyorObjectInstance` was missing all `PlatformObject`+`MvSonicOnPtfm2` solid profile overrides, defaulting `usesPreUpdatePositionForSolidContact()=false`. ROM `LCon_Platform` (routine 2, `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/63 LZ Conveyor.asm:149-153`) calls `PlatformObject` **before** `LCon_Platform_Update` which calls `SpeedToPos` (lines 191-232), so the contact check occurs at the pre-move position. The engine called `applyConveyorMovement()` first; with the default `false`, `ObjectSolidContactController` used the post-move position (y=0x2BD, 1px below pre-move y=0x2BE). The engine context at f6517 showed `eng-near s79 0x63 LZConveyor @0C52,02BD no-touch pre=@0C52,02BE` ‚ÄĒ the pre-update y=0x2BE was correct but the contact check fired at y=0x2BD. With `usesPreUpdatePositionForSolidContact()=true`, contact fires at y=0x2BE, Sonic's centre snaps to `0x2BE - 0x1C = 0x2A2`, matching ROM.
- Additional fixes (full `PlatformObject`+`MvSonicOnPtfm2` profile alignment with `Sonic1PlatformObjectInstance`): `HALF_HEIGHT` changed from 0x08 to 9 (`MvSonicOnPtfm2 subi.w #9,d0`; `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub MvSonicOnPtfm.asm:18-41`); `getTopLandingSnapAdjustment()=-1` (detect at obY-8; `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub PlatformObject.asm:17`); `rejectsZeroDistanceTopSolidLanding()=true` (UNSIGNED `cmpi.w #-16,d0 / blo`; `asm:21-22`); `usesCollisionHalfWidthForTopLanding()=true` (`LCon_Platform` passes `obActWid` directly to `PlatformObject` as d1; `asm:150-152`); `carriesAirborneRiderAfterExitPlatform()=true` (`LCon_OnPlatform` calls `MvSonicOnPtfm2` unconditionally after `ExitPlatform`; `asm:157-164`).
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): GHZ2 GREEN, SYZ2 GREEN. All other S1 frontiers byte-identical: GHZ1 f3246/255, GHZ3 f2693/477, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ3 f2079/1048, SBZ1 f2268/805, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SLZ1 f933/246, SLZ2 f1016/221, SYZ1 f816/351, SYZ3 f6065/483.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 LR spring used an exclusive right edge so a flush side contact never fired; SYZ1 f502 -> f816 (484 -> 351)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-syz1-advance` off `develop` cebf130d6.
- **`s1_syz1` advanced f502 -> f816, errors 484 -> 351.** First error before: f502 `camera_y` (a SYMPTOM; the driving divergence is `g_speed`/`x_speed` exp=0x1000 act=0x0000 ‚ÄĒ ROM spring-launched right, engine stopped). After: f816 `camera_x` (1px, a separate downstream frontier).
- Root (BizHawk + ROM physics.csv + engine debug; SYZ1 offset 61548, replay f502 = gfc 0x1F7): ROM launches Sonic RIGHT at 0x1000 via a horizontal (LR) spring (Obj41 subtype 0x10, @0218,03C2) ‚ÄĒ Sonic falls flush against the spring's right side and `Spring_LR` (after `SolidObject` sets Sonic's push bit) bounces him (x 022B->0223 via `subi #16,obX`/`neg obVelX`, xspd->0x1000). Deciding-question check (engine `getCentreY()` vs the trace physics.csv reference, anchored by `gameplay_frame_counter`): the fall positions MATCH ROM **frame-for-frame** and BOTH land at f502 at the same y=03BD ‚ÄĒ it is NOT a fall-timing or land-band off-by-one. The only difference is the land OUTCOME. The miss: the engine's SolidObject contact x-range gate (`ObjectSolidContactController`: `relX >= 2*halfWidth -> reject`) EXCLUDES the right edge, but ROM `SolidObject` `Solid_ChkCollision` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub SolidObject.asm:160-166`, `cmp d3,d0; bhi Solid_NoCollision`) rejects only when STRICTLY greater than `2*halfWidth` ‚ÄĒ the right edge (Sonic flush against the object's right face) still collides. The LR spring's right solid edge (`0218 + 19 = 022B`) equals Sonic's centre x (`022B`), so `relX == 2*halfWidth` and the contact was rejected; the spring side contact never fired, so `Spring_LR` never set the push bit and Sonic fell to terrain (xspd 0).
- Fix: the spring's `getSolidRoutineProfile()` now returns `fullSolid(usesStickyContactBuffer(), /*inclusiveRightEdge*/ true, false)`. The engine already has the `inclusiveRightEdge` `SolidRoutineProfile` flag (used `true` by `Sonic1GirderBlock`/`Junction`/`PushBlock`/`InvisibleBarrier`); the spring was using the single-arg `fullSolid` which defaults it `false`. Object-local to Obj41. Updated `TestSonic1SpringObjectInstance.exposesFullSolidRoutineProfileForVerticalAndHorizontalSprings` (it asserted `inclusiveRightEdge=false`; now `true`, ROM-cited).
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): all other S1 complete-runs byte-identical (GHZ1 f3246/255, GHZ3 f2693/477, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2578/1010, MZ3 f2079/1048, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, SLZ1 f933/246, SLZ2 f1016/221, SLZ3 f718/1073, SBZ1 f2268/805, SBZ2 f1395/1000, SYZ2 green, SYZ3 f3476/485, FZ f713/155). GHZ2 + SBZ3 GREEN. S2 EHZ1 green; S3K AIZ f1095/4309 identical. Unit tests: `CollisionSystemTest` 54/0, `TestSolidRoutineProfiles` 13/0, `TestHtzSpringLoop` 1/0, `TestSonic1SpringObjectInstance` 2/0 (updated).

## 2026-06-22 - S1 Electrocuter read the wrong frame counter for its zap gate; SBZ1 f1925 -> f2268 (997 -> 805)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-sbz1-advance` off `develop` 351a10476.
- **`s1_sbz1` advanced f1925 -> f2268, errors 997 -> 805.** First error before: f1925 `g_speed` exp=0x0556 act=0x0000 (rolling player zapped). After: f2268 `y_speed` (an unrelated VanishingPlatform / `onObj=0x53` object-landing miss ‚ÄĒ engine airborne, ROM grounded ‚ÄĒ a separate frontier).
- Root (BizHawk-confirmed, tools/bizhawk/sbz1_diag.lua): the SBZ Electrocuter (Obj6E, `Sonic1ElectrocuterObjectInstance`) gates its zap on ROM `Elec_Shock` `(v_framecount & elec_freq) == 0` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/6E SBZ Electrocuter.asm`); only `obFrame==4` of the zap animation sets the `col_144x16|col_hurt` ($A4) HURT box. The engine's elec was vfc-correct in isolation (capture: elec subtype 8 / freq 0x7F, zap triggers at v_framecount=1920, frame4 at v_framecount=1926 = trace 1925, ROM player hurt at v_framecount=1927 = trace 1926 via the normal 1-frame slot-order touch latency). But it resolved `v_framecount` from `objectManager.getFrameCounter()` ‚ÄĒ ObjectManager's OWN free-running counter, which is NOT seeded from the trace (`TraceReplaySessionBootstrap` seeds only LevelManager/Sprites). That counter ran +1 ahead of ROM `v_framecount`, so at trace 1925 the engine vfc was 1927 vs ROM 1926, and the frame-4 hurt landed one trace-frame early on the rolling player (engine player x matched ROM at every frame; only the vfc clock was +1).
- Fix: `Sonic1ElectrocuterObjectInstance.resolveVFrameCounter` now reads `services().levelManager().getFrameCounter() + 1` ‚ÄĒ `LevelManager.frameCounter` is the canonical trace-seeded `Level_frame_counter`, and objects execute with `frameCounter+1` (LevelManager.updateObjectPositions* pre-increment), so this equals the current ROM `v_framecount`. The elec's `update(frameCounter,...)` param is the VBla clock for S1 objects and cannot be used. Object-local (SBZ Electrocuter only).
- Regression sweep (vs develop baseline, first-error frame + count): SBZ2 f1395/1000 byte-identical (its frontier is an `obj_s48_slot` allocation before any zap), SBZ3 GREEN, GHZ1 f3246/255, GHZ2 GREEN, MZ1 f2089/205, SLZ1 f933/246 ‚ÄĒ all match. S2 EHZ1 green; S3K AIZ f1095/4309 identical. `TestObjectServicesMigrationGuard` passes. (No electrocuter unit test exists.) Latent sibling noted but untouched: `Sonic1RingInstance` also reads `objectManager.getFrameCounter()` for ring-sparkle timing (visual only).
## 2026-06-22 - S1 off-screen self-delete badnik respawn; MZ2 f2578 -> f2819

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-mz2-advance` (worktree off `develop`, base f5a6252bd).
- Command: `mvn -q "-Dtest=TestS1Mz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off" test`.
- **`s1_mz2` / `TestS1Mz2CompleteRunTraceReplay` advanced f2578 -> f2819.** First error before: f2578 `y_speed` expected=-0568 actual=0x0568 (clean sign flip, 1010 errors). After: f2819 `g_speed` expected=0x0018 actual=0x0000 (an unrelated downstream cluster, 1116 errors).
- Root (BizHawk-confirmed, `tools/bizhawk/mz2_diag.lua` + `mz2_cat_scan.lua`, ROM `s1.gen`): at f2578 the ROM has the MZ2 Caterkiller (Obj 0x78, layout entry x=0x0440) walking left, head at x=0x0414 in object slot 0x37 with 3 body segments (0x38-0x3A). Sonic, rolling and falling (`y_vel` +0x530+gravity = +0x568), rolls into the head; ROM `React_BadnikHit` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/Sonic ReactToItem.asm:298-305`) negates his falling `y_vel` to **-0x568** to bounce him up. The engine had **no Caterkiller at all** there (the temp trace diagnostic + CATSCAN showed the engine Caterkiller despawned ~f1065 after walking off-screen and never respawned), so Sonic fell through to terrain at f2581 ‚ÄĒ all 1010 errors cascaded from this.
- Why it never respawned: the Caterkiller owns its `out_of_range` tail (`usesCustomOutOfRangeCheck()=true`, `isCustomOutOfRange()` no-op) and deletes itself off-screen via `setDestroyedByOffscreen()` in its own routine (so its fragment-entry branch runs first). That reaches `ObjectManager`'s destroyed-removal path, which only called `placement.clearStayActive(spawn)` and left the counter-based respawn bit latched. ROM `Cat_Despawn` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/78 Badnik - Caterkiller.asm:139-148`) instead does `bclr #7,2(a2,d0.w)` into the `v_objstate` respawn table, re-enabling respawn. Fix: new `resetRespawnStateForOffscreenSelfDelete` helper, called from all six destroyed-removal sites, mirrors `unloadCounterBasedOutOfRange`'s respawnable reset (`clearCounterForSpawn` + `markDormant`) but only when `isDestroyedRespawnable()` (off-screen) and `clearsRespawnStateOnCounterBasedOutOfRange()`. Player kills (`setDestroyed`, `destroyedRespawnable=false`) keep the bit set. Gated on `placement.isCounterBasedRespawn()` -> S1-only.
- Regression sweep (with-fix isolated vs documented/`git`-baseline first-error frame + count): MZ2 f2578/1010 -> f2819/1116 (advance). GHZ1 f3246/255, GHZ3 f2693/477, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ3 f2079 (1048 -> 1123, same first-error frame, within its pre-existing post-f2079 cascade), LZ1 f5745, SBZ1 f1925, SYZ1 f502/484 ‚ÄĒ first-error frames UNCHANGED. GHZ2 stays GREEN. S2/S3K untouched (non-counter placement). Unit tests PASS: TestObjectManagerCounterBasedDynamicUnload (11), TestObjectPlacementControllerS1Counter, TestS1CounterPlacementRememberedParity, TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn, TestBlueBallsBackwardApproachRespawn, TestSonic1CaterkillerBodyChaining (6), TestSonic1LavaGeyserOutOfRange (4), TestSolidRoutineProfiles (13); S3K must-keep-green (TestSonic3kLevelLoading 30, TestSonic3kBootstrapResolver 5) + S1/S2 credits pass. No frontier regressed.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 GHZ collapsing-ledge top-landing width advances GHZ1 f2790 -> f3246 (436 -> 255)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-s1-camera-vscroll` (worktree off current `develop`, which carries the GHZ2-green camera fix 99557976c; develop confirmed ancestor).
- Command: `mvn -q -o "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.*TraceReplay,TestCamera,..." "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off" test`.
- GHZ1 before: f2790 `camera_y` (the report's first-error field, but a SYMPTOM) ‚ÄĒ the real divergence is the player: ROM y=0x027C ys=0 g=0x0360 air=0 onObj=0x51 vs engine y=0x027F ys=0x00E0 air=1 (still falling). 436 errors. After: f3246 `y` (1px on an Obj18 platform ride), 255 errors. Frontier advanced ~456 frames; -181 errors.
- Root: GHZ start has two collapsing ledges (Obj1A) in sequence. Sonic runs off the first (engine RIDING=s3B until f2784), falls f2785-2789, and ROM lands him on the SECOND collapsing ledge at f2790 (onObj 0x24 -> 0x51). The engine did not seat him until f2793 (RIDING=s50) ‚ÄĒ 3 frames late ‚ÄĒ so he kept falling and the trajectory diverged forward. Engine-side TEMP log in `resolveContactInternal` showed the new-landing was `REJECT-width`: at f2790 distY=6 (inside the [1,0x0F] band) but `isWithinTopLandingWidth=false` because the engine narrowed the landing half-width to `0x30 - 0x0B = 0x25` (the generic S2 `SolidObjectFull`/`Solid_Landed` `obActWid` narrowing). relX=2 -> d1 = (2-0x30)+0x25 = -9 < 0 -> reject; only relX=12 (f2793) passed.
- ROM: `Ledge_ChkTouch` (routine 2, new-landing) does `move.w #96/2,d1` (= 0x30) then `bsr SlopeObject` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/1A, 53 Collapsing Ledges and Floors.asm:31-33`). `SlopeObject` runs the X-range check on that `d1` directly with NO narrowing (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub PlatformObject.asm:133-139`: `add d1,d0; bmi exit; add d1,d1; cmp d1,d0; bhs exit`). So the standable top-landing half-width is the full 0x30, and the engine's `-$B` narrowing is wrong for this S1 SlopeObject path.
- Fix: `Sonic1CollapsingLedgeObjectInstance.usesCollisionHalfWidthForTopLanding()` -> true (one object-local override, 16 lines incl. comment). Matches the sibling `Sonic1CollapsingFloorObjectInstance` which already opts in (CFlo_ChkTouch passes #64/2 directly). Object-local to Obj1A; GHZ-only spawn.
- Regression sweep (this branch vs develop baseline, first-error frame + error count): GHZ1 f2790/436 -> f3246/255 (advance). GHZ2 stays GREEN (0). All other S1 byte-identical: GHZ3 f2693/477, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2578/1010, MZ3 f2079/1048, SBZ1 f1925/997, SBZ2 f1395/1035, SLZ1 f723/661, SLZ2 f1016/221, SLZ3 f718/1073, SYZ1 f502/484, SYZ3 f3476/485, SYZ2 green, FZ f713/155, all 8 credits green. S2 EHZ1 pass, CPZ f3365/310 identical. S3K AIZ f1095/4309, ICZ f3139/3179, LBZ f2270/5881 identical; TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless green. Camera units pass (TestCamera 26, TestCameraRewindSnapshot 3, TestLookScrollDelay 5, TestSonic3kCnzScroll 6). No frontier regressed.
- NOTE on the original task: GHZ1 f2790 and LZ1 f5745 (both "camera_y" first-error) are NOT the camera vertical-settle bug ‚ÄĒ they are player object-landing misses (engine drops Sonic airborne over a solid ROM keeps him on); camera_y is a downstream symptom. MZ1 f2089 IS the pure camera vsettle (6px, player matches) and stays deferred (non-greenable f4230 cascade; high GHZ2-regression risk; see [[mz1-f2089-camera-boundary-accel-transition]]).
## 2026-06-22 - S1 Bomb fuse decremented bom_time on its spawn frame; SLZ1 f723->f933, errors 661->246

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-slz1-advance` off `develop` a6fc977fc.
- **`s1_slz1` advanced f723 -> f933, errors 661 -> 246.** First error before: f723 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=-0200 (player hurt-bounce). After: f933 `y` 1px (a separate downstream frontier).
- Root (BizHawk-confirmed, tools/bizhawk/slz1_diag.lua): the S1 Walking Bomb FUSE (Obj5F sub4, `Sonic1BombFuseInstance`) decremented its `bom_time` on its OWN creation frame, so it expired and spawned the 4 shrapnel one frame early. The whole 38-frame shrapnel flight shifted back one frame, hurting the (ducking) player at trace f723 instead of ROM's f724. ROM holds `bom_time=143` at the end of the fuse's creation frame (bk2 f137203) ‚ÄĒ the FindNextFreeObj fuse slot is not executed by ExecuteObjects on the frame it is created ‚ÄĒ then counts down and expires when `0 -> -1` (`subq.w #1,bom_time; bmi`) at f137347. Verified by frame-stamped engine logs aligned to the player-hurt anchor: engine fuse expiry trace f685 vs ROM f686; both fuse-to-hurt spans 38 frames (trajectory identical, just shifted one frame).
- Disproven en route: (a) deferring the secondary shrapnel pieces' first move ‚ÄĒ engaged but ZERO change (shrapnel trajectory was already correct relative to its early spawn); (b) the shared S1 inline player-touch pre-update snapshot ‚ÄĒ the capture showed the fuse itself fires early, not the touch phase.
- Fix: `Sonic1BombFuseInstance.skipsSameFrameUpdateAfterSpawn()` -> `true`, ROM-cited (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/5F Badnik - Walking Bomb.asm` Bom_CheckStartFuse / Bom_BurnFuseAndExplode). Routes the fuse through `addDynamicObjectNextFrame` so it does not update on its creation frame, reproducing the ROM creation-frame hold. Object-local (S1 Walking Bomb only).
- Regression sweep (vs `develop` baseline, first-error frame + count): all other S1 complete-runs byte-identical (GHZ1 f2790/436, GHZ3 f2693/477, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2578/1010, MZ3 f2079/1048, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, SLZ2 f1016/221, SLZ3 f718/1073, SYZ1 f502/484, SYZ2 f6845/55, SYZ3 f3476/399, FZ f713/155, SBZ1 f1925/997). GHZ2 + SBZ3 GREEN. SBZ2 IMPROVED 1035 -> 1000 (same first-error frame f1395 ‚ÄĒ SBZ also uses the Walking Bomb). S2 EHZ1 green; S3K AIZ f1095/4309 identical. S1 bomb-child rewind tests pass (TestS1BadnikChildGraphRewind, TestS1BadnikGenericRecreate, TestRewindFixS1Batch2Codecs, TestSpawnRewindRecreatableCleanup). Pre-existing (not this change): `TestNoServicesInObjectConstructors` fails on `SkidDustObjectInstance` (S3K, another branch's WIP) ‚ÄĒ fails identically with this fix stashed.

## 2026-06-22 - DEZ jet-stomp reads targeting sensor 1 frame late; DEZ1 f5261->f5952, errors 98->46

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-dez1-green` (builds on the group-anim $C0 + boss-id boundary fixes below).
- **`s2_dez1` advanced f5261 -> f5952, errors 98 -> 46, roots 5 -> 3.** First error before: f5261 `y_speed` sign-flip (jet-stomp bounce miss). After: f5952 `y_speed` exp=0x0098 act=-0098 (a later jet-stomp; small residual drift remains).
- Root: BizHawk phase-alignment of the jet-stomp cycle (engine fc = trace-1) showed, after the $C0 fix, the body's pa06(spawn)->pa0A(recovery) span was 227 frames vs ROM 228 ‚ÄĒ the engine lost 1 frame in the descent/sensor region per jet-stomp, re-drifting the attack clock (ascent-start őĒ +1/0/-1/-2 across jet-stomps). The body's wait-for-sensor phase updated `ChildObjC7_TargettingSensor` inline and read `targetedPlayerX` the SAME frame, so the descent began the frame the sensor reported. ROM `loc_3D784` reads the body's `objoff_28` at the body's frame start, but the sensor is a higher-slot object that runs AFTER the body in ExecuteObjects and writes `objoff_28` on its lock-on-report frame (`loc_3DE62`), so the body observes the report ONE frame later.
- Fix: capture `targetedPlayerX` BEFORE advancing the sensor child, then advance the sensor (for next frame). Restores the one-frame report latency; the descent starts on ROM's frame. Errors 98 -> 46.
- Regression: object-local (`Sonic2DeathEggRobotInstance`, DEZ-only). Residual: a smaller attack-clock drift (~1 frame / 2 jet-stomps) still misses the f5952 bounce; under investigation.

## 2026-06-22 - DEZ Silver Sonic does not clear Current_Boss_ID; DEZ1 f4933->f5261 (roots 13->5)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-dez1-green` (worktree off `develop`, builds on the group-anim $C0 fix below).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS2DezEndingLevelSelectTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- **`s2_dez1` advanced f4933 -> f5261, non-cascading roots 13 -> 5** (errors held at 98; the f4933 boundary cluster cleared, remaining roots are the residual boss-bounce sensor-report drift at later jet-stomps). First error before: f4933 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=0x02DC. After: f5261 `y_speed` exp=-0178 act=0x0178.
- Root: at f4933 BizHawk shows the boss body UP HIGH @077E,0024 (ascending ‚ÄĒ not touching the player); the player runs RIGHT post-bounce and ROM clamps x at 0x868 (xv->0, becomes pushing) while the engine kept x_speed=0x2DC and sailed past. 0x868 is the DEZ arena right player boundary = `Camera_Max_X`(0x740, set by Sonic2DEZEvents arena lock) + 0x128 (`screen_width-24`). ROM `Sonic_LevelBound` applies that STRICT boundary (no `+$40` lenient extension) iff `Current_Boss_ID != 0` (`s2.asm:37244-37251`). ROM Silver Sonic sets `Current_Boss_ID=9` at its arena lock (`s2.asm:77528`) and NO S2 boss ever clears it, so it stays 9 through the Death Egg Robot fight -> strict boundary throughout. The engine's `Sonic2MechaSonicInstance` defeat called `setCurrentBossId(0)`, reverting to the lenient `+$40` boundary (0x8A8) for the whole Death Egg Robot fight; the player wasn't clamped at the arena edge. Confirmed by instrumenting `doLevelBoundary`: at predX 0x855-0x868, maxX=0x740 (correct) but strict=false / boss=false -> rightBoundary 0x8A8.
- Fix: remove the premature `setCurrentBossId(0)` from `Sonic2MechaSonicInstance.updateDefeat` so `Current_Boss_ID` stays 9 through the Death Egg Robot fight, matching ROM (which never clears it). The engine's existing boss-strict gating (`PlayableSpriteMovement.doLevelBoundary`, `RightBoundary.compute`) is already ROM-faithful ‚ÄĒ it just needed the flag held. DEZ-only object; the post-defeat ending walk (Camera_Max_X opens to 0x1000) stays unblocked (target 0xEC0 < 0x1000+0x128).
- Regression: DEZ-only (MechaSonic spawns only in DEZ). No shared code changed. The remaining 5 roots (f5261/f5462/f5952/f6231) are all the residual ~1-frame sensor lock-on / descent-handoff drift at later jet-stomps; tracked for follow-up.

## 2026-06-22 - DEZ Death Egg Robot group-anim end-marker frame collapses DEZ1 127->98, advances f4007->f4933

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-dez1-green` (worktree off `develop`).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS2DezEndingLevelSelectTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- **`s2_dez1` / `TestS2DezEndingLevelSelectTraceReplay` advanced f4007 -> f4933, errors 127 -> 98.** First error before: f4007 `y_speed`/`x_speed` velocity sign-flips (Death Egg Robot bounce miss). First error after: f4933 `x_speed` exp=0x0000 act=0x02DC (a later attack; residual 1-frame sensor-report drift).
- Root: the boss group-animation player `Sonic2DeathEggRobotInstance.stepGroupAnimation` returned "script complete" on the same frame the last keyframe's substeps finished, but ROM `ObjC7_GroupAni` (`loc_3E1AA`) terminates each script with a `$C0` end-marker keyframe processed on a SEPARATE frame (reads `anim_frame` at frame start: last real substep frame N advances `anim_frame` to `$C0`; frame N+1 reads `$C0` -> `loc_3E23E`/`loc_3E27E`/`loc_3E236` returns done with no deltas). So every ROM script costs one extra completion frame (off_3E3D0 crouch `0,1,2,$C0` = 41f not 40f; off_3E30A walk `0..8,$C0`). The engine omitted the marker, so every group-anim-gated attack-phase transition (crouch, walk-punch, stand-up walk) advanced one frame early. BizHawk-measured: the engine attack clock drifted progressively behind ROM (jet-stomp ascent-start őĒ -2/-5/-7 across attacks), so by the f4007 jet-stomp the targeting-sensor lock-on snapped 7-8 frames early (engine trace f3877 vs ROM f3885, player moving left ~240/frame), the descent stomped at x=0x815 vs ROM 0x80E, and the body sat ~+5x/+8y of ROM at the bounce frame, missing the player's roll-up bounce box (`Touch_Enemy` neg.w x_vel/y_vel, s2.asm:85318-85345).
- Fix: model the `$C0` end-marker frame ‚ÄĒ when the substep advance lands `groupAnimFrameIdx == sequence.length`, fall through WITHOUT returning true (no deltas; last real keyframe's deltas already applied this frame); the next call's top-of-method end check returns done. Re-measured: jet-stomp ascent-start őĒ collapses to 0/+1 through f4007, the f4007 bounce connects. Object-local to `Sonic2DeathEggRobotInstance` (the Death Egg Robot spawns only in DEZ), so no other zone/trace is affected.
- Regression: object-local change; no shared code touched. CNZ1 first-error held at its develop baseline f1691 (downstream count only); EHZ1/ARZ1/MCZ1/SCZ/WFZ stay green; S1 GHZ2 untouched (S1, different game). No frontier regressed.
- Residual (NOT in this commit): f4933 onward, a later jet-stomp still has a ~1-frame sensor lock-on report drift (the body ph06->ph08 descent-start reads the sensor's reported X one frame off ROM's pa06->pa08 handoff). Separate sensor/body update-order alignment; tracked for a follow-up. See memory `dez1-f4007-boss-body-position`.
## 2026-06-22 - S1 moving-platform pre-move walk-off bounds + exit carry GREENS SYZ2 (TestS1Syz2CompleteRunTraceReplay 0 errors)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-syz2-advance` (worktree off current `develop`; develop confirmed ancestor; follow-on to the headroom-double-flip entry below that advanced SYZ2 to f6845).
- Command: `mvn -q -o "-Dtest=com.openggf.tests.trace.s1.TestS1Syz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off" test`.
- SYZ2 before: f6845 `x` exp=0x211A actual=0x211C (55 errors). After: 0 errors (GREEN). Frontier f6845 -> green; error count 55 -> 0.
- Root cause: at f6844 Sonic lands (from a rolling jump arc) onto a horizontally-oscillating Obj18 platform (slot 0x22 engine / stand_on_obj 0x25 ROM) that is sliding LEFT ~2px/frame. ROM keeps him grounded on it through f6846 (obj=25) and carries his x by the platform delta; the engine dropped the ride at f6845 (one frame early) and skipped the carry, going +6px (full x_speed) instead of +4px, then airborne a frame ahead of ROM. Two linked ROM-order mismatches in `ObjectSolidContactController.processInlineRidingObject`: (1) the continued-ride walk-off bounds used the platform POST-move x; ROM `ExitPlatform` (routine 4 `Plat_Action2`) runs BEFORE `Plat_Move` and checks the PRE-move x. (2) the out-of-bounds exit path skipped the final `MvSonicOnPtfm2` carry; ROM `MvSonicOnPtfm2` does not test the on-object bit and still carries the just-exited rider by the post-move delta.
- Engine-side decisive evidence (TEMP riding/platform log, now removed): f6845 SonicX=0x211C, platform pre-move=0x20FD (relX 0x3F < 0x40 -> stays) vs post-move=0x20FB (relX 0x41 -> spurious exit). With fix #1: f6845 carries -2px -> x 0x211A (matches ROM). Residual f6846 ROM x=0x211F vs un-carried 0x2120 closed by fix #2.
- ROM refs: `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/18 Platforms.asm:74-87` (Plat_Action2 order ExitPlatform -> Plat_Move -> MvSonicOnPtfm2), `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub ExitPlatform.asm:20-27` (pre-move bounds), `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub MvSonicOnPtfm.asm:18-41` (unconditional carry), `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub PlatformObject.asm` (standing).
- Fix: `ObjectSolidContactController` exit-bounds reference = `ridingX` (pre-move) when `provider.usesPreUpdatePositionForSolidContact(player)`; plus a final `currentX-ridingX` carry on the out-of-bounds exit path when `carriesAirborneRiderAfterExitPlatform() && carriesRiderOnHorizontalMove()`. One file. Both gated on existing provider predicates (S1 Obj18/52/59; S3K CnzCylinder/MGZTopPlatform also set usesPreUpdate).
- Regression sweep (fix vs clean baseline, first-error frame + error count): SYZ2 f6845/55 -> GREEN. GHZ2 stays GREEN. GHZ1 f2790/436, GHZ3 f2693/477, LZ1 f5745/2185, LZ2 f1068/2150, LZ3 f6517/1726, MZ1 f2089/205, MZ2 f2578/1010, MZ3 f2079/1048, SBZ1 f1925/997, SBZ2 f1395/1035, SLZ1 f723/661, SLZ2 f1016/221, SLZ3 f718/1073, SYZ1 f502/484, SYZ3 f3476/485 ‚ÄĒ all byte-identical. Affected S3K objects (CnzCylinder/MGZTopPlatform): CNZ-CR f1846/6486 and MGZ-CR f866/7976 byte-identical clean-vs-fixed (verified by git-stash A/B). No frontier regressed.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 headroom ceiling-probe double-flip advances SYZ2 f1088 -> f6845

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-syz2-advance` (worktree off current `develop`; develop confirmed ancestor).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Syz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false -Ds1.rom.path=s1.gen "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- SYZ2 before: f1088 `x_speed` exp=0x02E8 actual=0x02F4 (311 errors). After: f6845 `x` exp=0x211A actual=0x211C (55 errors). Frontier advanced ~5757 frames; error count 311 -> 55.
- Root cause: at f1088 the BK2 input adds the jump button (0x08 -> 0x18) and ROM jumps (y_speed -0680, air/rolling) while the engine keeps ground-accelerating (x_speed 0x02E8 -> 0x02F4). The engine detected the jump press but `doJump()` was blocked: `getTerrainHeadroomDistance` returned 3 (< the 6px `Sonic_Jump` gate, `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/01 Sonic.asm:1180-1190`). The left ceiling probe hits SYZ2 collision tile 0x0093 (a left overhang; `rom[0x62A00+0x93*16]` = F7 F8 F9 FA FC FD FE 00..., verified identical to the engine's loaded tile by reading the ROM file directly ‚ÄĒ NOT a load bug). The headroom CalcRoomOverHead quadrant-0x80 probe pre-applied the `eori.w #$F` ceiling flip as a `dy` offset (`xorDelta`), but the engine's `Direction.UP` `scanVertical`/`calculateVerticalDistance` already models that flip ‚ÄĒ so it was DOUBLE-applied, yielding distance 3 instead of 8.
- BizHawk capture (this is the decisive evidence; bk2 offset 71507, f72595 = trace f1088; `Sonic_FindCeiling` hooks at 0x156CE/0x156D2): ROM obX=0x074F, obWidth=9, left probe d3=0x0746 column 6 ‚ÄĒ ALL identical to the engine ‚ÄĒ but ROM `leftDist(d1)=8 rightDist(d0)=7` (min 7 >= 6 -> JUMPS). ROM `FindFloor` (`sub FindNearestTile & FindFloor & FindWall.asm:163-167`) negates the -2 ceiling height to +2 and returns `15 - (2 + (probeY&$F=5)) = 8`. The engine's UP path returns exactly 8 WITHOUT the pre-flip.
- Fix: `CollisionSystem.describeCalcRoomOverHeadProbes` quadrant 0x80 drops the `xorDelta` Y pre-offset (`dy=0`), letting `scanVertical` own the single ceiling flip ‚ÄĒ matching the ordinary ceiling-sensor path. One file, `src/main/java/com/openggf/physics/CollisionSystem.java`. Shared S1/S2/S3K ceiling-probe code; game-agnostic.
- Regression sweep (fix vs clean detached-`develop` baseline, first-error frame + error count): SYZ2 f1088/311 -> f6845/55 (advance). GHZ3 f2693/492 -> f2693/477 (same frame, FEWER errors). GHZ1 f2790/436, LZ1 f5745/2185, SLZ1 f723/661, SLZ3 f718/1073, SLZ2 f1016/221 ‚ÄĒ byte-identical. GHZ2 stays GREEN (0 errors). MZ1 first-error frame UNCHANGED at f2089; total count 185 -> 205, entirely within its pre-existing f4230+ physics cascade (a region already fully divergent and non-greenable; no new earlier divergence). S2 EHZ1 green, ARZ green, CPZ f3365/310 identical. S3K AIZ f1095/4309, ICZ f3139/3179, LBZ f2270/5881 identical; TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless passes. No frontier regressed.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 camera bottom-boundary follow GREENS GHZ2 (TestS1Ghz2CompleteRunTraceReplay 0 errors)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-s1-camera-vsettle` (worktree off current `develop`; develop confirmed ancestor). Base already carries the Obj18 platform-jump fix (develop b69c2c6ca) that took GHZ2 to f3349.
- Command: `mvn -Dmse=off test "-Dtest=TestS1Ghz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false -Ds1.rom.path=s1.gen "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- GHZ2 before: f3349 `camera_y` expected=0x034A actual=0x034C (2 errors). After: **GREEN, 0 errors.** GHZ2 is the first S1 complete-run trace to go fully green.
- Root cause: the engine's vertical camera lagged a rising bottom level boundary by one frame. When the player rolls fast into a new lower area, `DynamicLevelEvents` (`docs/s1disasm/_inc/DynamicLevelEvents.asm:5-49`) eases `v_limitbtm2` toward `v_limitbtm1` at 2px/frame and sets `f_bgscrollvert=1` BEFORE `ScrollVertical`. ROM `ScrollVertical` then branches `SV_OnGround` -> (f_bgscrollvert set) `SV_BottomBoundaryMoving` (`docs/s1disasm/_inc/ScrollHoriz & ScrollVertical.asm:157-158,210`) -> `SV_SweetSpot` -> `SV_BottomBoundary` (line 259), clamping the camera to the freshly-moved boundary EVEN on a sweet-spot frame where the normal scroll produced no movement. The engine's `Camera.updatePosition` bottom-boundary clamp was gated only on `y != yBeforeVerticalScroll`, so on a sweet-spot frame (vertical scroll produced no movement) it skipped the clamp and the camera held its old y while `maxY` (engine mirror of `v_limitbtm2`) had already eased past it. BizHawk capture (bk2 offset 6622, ScrollVertical hook at 0x69AA + boundary poll): boundary eases 0x0400 -> 0x0300 under a grounded fast roll-land (g_speed ~0x0DE1, rolling); ROM camY reaches 0x034A at the divergence frame while the engine held 0x034C because its sweet-spot frame skipped the boundary clamp.
- Fix: `Camera.updatePosition` bottom-boundary clamp condition widened from `if (!lastFrameWrapped && y != yBeforeVerticalScroll)` to `if (!lastFrameWrapped && (y != yBeforeVerticalScroll || maxYChanging))`. `maxYChanging` is the engine's existing `f_bgscrollvert` mirror, set by `updateBoundaryEasing` (called before `updatePosition`). Shared S1/S2/S3K `ScrollVertical` behaviour; game-agnostic (no zone/game branch).
- Regression sweep (this branch vs clean detached-`develop` baseline, first-error frame + error count): S1 GHZ1 f2790/436, GHZ3 f2693/537, MZ1 f2089/185, LZ1 f5745/2185, SLZ1 f723/661, SLZ3 f718/1073 ‚ÄĒ ALL byte-identical. S2 EHZ1 green, ARZ level-select green, CPZ level-select f3365 `tails_x`/310 ‚ÄĒ identical. S3K AIZ complete-run f1095/4309, ICZ complete-run f3139/3179, LBZ complete-run f2270/5881 ‚ÄĒ ALL byte-identical. Must-keep-green PASS: TestS3kAiz1SkipHeadless, TestSonic3kLevelLoading, TestSonic3kBootstrapResolver, TestSonic3kDecodingUtils, TestS1Credits00Ghz1, TestS1Credits07Ghz1b, TestS1Ghz1, TestS1Mz1. Camera unit tests PASS: TestCamera (26), TestCameraRewindSnapshot (3), TestLookScrollDelay (5), TestSonic3kCnzScroll (6), TestAizMinibossCameraUnlock. No frontier regressed; GHZ2 GREEN.
- Note: the related camera_y first-errors in GHZ1 (f2790)/MZ1 (f2089)/LZ1 (f5745) are DIFFERENT camera issues (not the boundary-follow lag) and are unchanged by this fix.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 Obj18 platform jump-off post-move re-seat advances GHZ2 to f3349 (also GHZ3/GHZ1)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-ghz2-green` (worktree off current `develop`; develop confirmed ancestor).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Ghz2CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false -Ds1.rom.path=s1.gen "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- GHZ2 first error before: f2591 `y` expected=0x0259 actual=0x0257 (4 errors total). First error after: f3349 `camera_y` expected=0x034A actual=0x034C (2 errors total). Status: STILL RED at the new (unrelated, pre-existing) frontier ‚ÄĒ GHZ2 is **not** green. The f2591 `y` and its true f2597 `camera_y` cascade are cleared; the divergence report's prior `cascading: True` flag on f3349/f3366 was a heuristic misclassification (verified by BizHawk capture ‚ÄĒ they are independent).
- Root cause (f2591, FIXED): on the jump-off frame Sonic rides an Obj18 GHZ platform (slot 0x29). ROM `Plat_Action2` (routine 4, `docs/s1disasm/_incObj/18 Platforms.asm:74-87`) runs `ExitPlatform` (detaches the airborne rider) -> `Plat_Move`/`Plat_Nudge` (platform moves up) -> UNCONDITIONALLY `MvSonicOnPtfm2` (`docs/s1disasm/_incObj/sub MvSonicOnPtfm.asm:18`). `MvSonicOnPtfm2` does not check rider velocity, so it re-seats Sonic to `platformY-9-obHeight` using the POST-move platform y, overwriting `Sonic_Jump`'s `addq.w #sonic_height-sonic_roll_height,obY(a0)` (+5; `sonic.asm:1228`). BizHawk capture (bk2 offset 6622): platform `obY` 0x026E->0x0270, height 0x13->0x0E, player `y` 0x0252 -> 0x0257 (+5) -> 0x0259 (`MvSonicOnPtfm2`). The engine applied only the +5 and skipped the post-jump pull-up, leaving Sonic 2 px high.
- Fix: `Sonic1PlatformObjectInstance` opts into `carriesAirborneRiderAfterExitPlatform()` (the same opt-in Obj52 `Sonic1MovingBlockObjectInstance` / Obj59 `Sonic1ElevatorObjectInstance` already use; the engine carry lives in `ObjectSolidContactController.processInlineRidingObject`/`applyRidingCarry`). Its `update()` was restructured to a single POST-move solid checkpoint (mirroring `Sonic1MovingBlockObjectInstance`) so the airborne carry re-seats to the platform's moved y, not its pre-move y; the bob ramp now gates on the prior-frame `playerStanding` latch. Object-local change only ‚ÄĒ no shared-collision or camera code touched.
- Remaining GHZ2 blocker (NOT fixed): f3349 + f3366 `camera_y` 2 px, a pre-existing shared-`Camera.ScrollVertical` vertical-settle-timing bug. Player is grounded+rolling at high g_speed (~0x0DE1) after a fast fall+land; BizHawk shows the ROM camera in the sweet spot (d0=96, ~stationary) f3346-3349 while the engine holds `camY=0x034C` one frame longer than ROM (which moves to 0x034A). This same camera-model gap dominates GHZ1's remaining 436 errors. Out of scope here (shared camera, high cross-trace regression risk); flagged as the next target.
- Regression sweep (this branch vs clean detached-`develop` baseline, first-error frame + error count): GHZ1 f2790 `camera_y` 537 -> 436 (same frame, fewer errors ‚ÄĒ same Obj18 carry); GHZ3 baseline f1246 `y` -> f2693 `x` (frontier ADVANCED; f1246 was the same platform-jump-y family); SLZ1 f723 `x_speed`/661, SLZ3 f718 `y_speed`/1073, MZ1 f2089 `camera_y`/185, LZ1 f5745 `camera_y`/2185 ‚ÄĒ ALL byte-identical to baseline. Must-keep-green PASS: `TestS1Ghz1TraceReplay`, `TestS1Credits00Ghz1`, `TestS1Credits07Ghz1b`. Unit tests PASS (63): `TestS1JumpFromElevator` (analogous carry), `TestSonic1PlatformObjectInstanceRespawn`, `TestSolidRoutineProfiles`, `TestSonic1LargeGrassyPlatformObjectInstance`, `TestS1SimpleObjectGenericRecreate`. No frontier regressed.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 collapsing-floor landing surface advances MZ3 to f2079 (+ SLZ2 to f1016)

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-mz3-green` (worktree off `develop`, base `820350cf6`).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Mz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- **`s1_mz3` / `TestS1Mz3CompleteRunTraceReplay` advanced f1702 -> f2079.** First error before: f1702 `y` expected=0x048C actual=0x048B (span=1, 1071 errors; survey's "1 error" count was truncated ‚ÄĒ full count is 1071). First error after: f2079 `y_speed` expected=-02C8 actual=-03C8 (1048 errors) ‚ÄĒ an unrelated downstream jump-release `y_speed` cluster, not a landing regression.
- Root cause: at MZ3 f1701 (BizHawk 45145) Sonic falls rolling onto an MZ CollapsingFloor (Obj 0x53). ROM `CFlo_ChkTouch` -> `PlatformObject` detects the landing against the `obY-8` entry surface (`subq.w #8,d0`) and snaps him on that frame; the next frame (f1702 / BizHawk 45146) `CFlo_OnPlatform` -> `CFlo_WalkOff` -> `MvSonicOnPtfm2` re-seats him to the standing `obY-9` surface = `platY(0x04A8) - 9 - obHeight(19) = 0x048C`. The engine modeled BOTH the detect/snap and the riding surface at `obY-9` (`SolidObjectParams` ground-half-height 9, no `getTopLandingSnapAdjustment`), so its landing detection fired one frame late: at f1702 the engine was still mid-landing-transition at `0x048B` and only reached the ride surface `0x048C` at f1703. Verified by BizHawk capture (45145 ROM LAND `d2=0x0491` rolling obHeight=14; 45146 ROM `MvSonicOnPtfm2 -> 0x048C` standing obHeight=19) and engine per-frame trace (engine `centreY` 0x048B at f1702, 0x048C at f1703 ‚ÄĒ exactly one frame behind ROM).
- Fix: `Sonic1CollapsingFloorObjectInstance` opts into `getTopLandingSnapAdjustment() = -1` (recovers the `obY-8` detect/snap surface from the `obY-9` solid params for new landings, while continued riding keeps `obY-9`), `rejectsZeroDistanceTopSolidLanding()` (ROM `Plat_NoXCheck_AltY` unsigned `cmpi.w #-16,d0`/`blo` excludes the exact-touch `d0=0`; band is `d0 in [-16,-1]`), and `usesCollisionHalfWidthForTopLanding()` (`CFlo_ChkTouch` passes `#64/2` directly as PlatformObject's `d1`, no generic `+$B` narrowing). Object-local change only ‚ÄĒ no shared-collision/controller code touched. Same fix family as Obj18 `Sonic1PlatformObjectInstance` and the LZ Conveyor, which use the identical `PlatformObject` (land) + `MvSonicOnPtfm2` (ride) routine pair.
- Regression sweep (this branch vs clean stashed-`develop` baseline, first-error frame is the metric): **SLZ2 ADVANCED f651 `g_speed` -> f1016** (same obj-0x53 landing-surface root: ROM `PlatformObject -> Plat_NoCheck` sets `g_speed := x_speed` on the landing frame, which the late landing delayed). MZ1 f2089 `camera_y`, MZ2 f2578 `y_speed`, SLZ3 f718 `y_speed`, SBZ1 f1925 `g_speed` ‚ÄĒ first-error frames UNCHANGED. GHZ/non-collapsing-floor traces unaffected (no shared code touched). No frontier regressed.
- Next MZ3 blocker (NOT in this commit): f2079 jump-release `y_speed` cluster (constant ~0x100 offset, Sonic jumping off the collapsing floor) ‚ÄĒ a separate root, not the landing-surface family.

## 2026-06-22 - S1 right-wall odd-angle snap advances LZ3 to f6517

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-lz3-green` (worktree off `develop`, base `346483a2b`).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS1Lz3CompleteRunTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- **`s1_lz3` / `TestS1Lz3CompleteRunTraceReplay` advanced f1415 -> f6517.** First error before: f1415 `angle` expected=0x00C0 actual=0x00D0 (span=1, 3229 errors). First error after: f6517 `y_speed` expected=0x0000 actual=0x0860 (1726 errors) ‚ÄĒ an unrelated downstream LZ-conveyor landing miss, not an angle regression.
- Root cause: at LZ3 f1415 Sonic is in RIGHTWALL ground mode (`gm=3`) with both ground sensors returning zero distance and the flagged angle `0xFF`. ROM `Sonic_Angle` (docs/s1disasm/_incObj/Sonic AnglePos.asm:186-208) selects the nearer sensor and, when the selected angle has bit 0 set (`btst #0,d2 / bne .snap_angle`), snaps the angle straight from the *current* `obAngle` via `(obAngle + 0x20) & 0xC0` = 0xC0. There is no ROM-visible cross-frame alternate-angle cache. The engine's `CollisionSystem.selectSensorWithAngle` kept a `pendingOddSensorFallbackAngles` map (introduced by a non-ROM heuristic) that, on a RIGHTWALL zero-distance odd-angle frame, resurrected a stale even `0xD0` remembered from a prior frame's alternate sensor instead of running the ROM cardinal snap ‚ÄĒ reverting the player angle to `0xD0` for one frame (the ROM stays `0xC0` from f1413 on; the engine matched until f1415 then briefly reverted).
- Fix: remove the entire `pendingOddSensorFallbackAngles` / `usesOddRightWallFallback` / `applyAngleFromSelectedSensor` / `rememberOddSensorFallback` mechanism and let `selectSensorWithAngle` call the plain `applyAngleFromSensor` (which already does the ROM odd-angle `(angle+0x20)&0xC0` snap). Shared S1/S3K right-wall ground-angle path; not gated on zone/route/frame. `CollisionSystemTest` 53/0.
- Duplicate note: this is the identical change as sibling commit `1f4fb901f` ("align right-wall odd angle snap", discovered via S3K CNZ f2619) on branch `bugfix/ai-trace-frontier-clusters`, which is NOT on `develop`. Landing it here is correct; if that branch later merges, git sees the change already present.
- Regression sweep (staged angle-only vs `develop` baseline, first-error frame is the metric): S3K `TestS3kCnzCompleteRunTraceReplay` first error held at f1846 `tails_x_speed` (the angle fix does not block CNZ complete-run; the CNZ `angle` root lives in the window-based `TestS3kCnzTraceReplay`). S1 GHZ1 f2790 / GHZ2 f2591 / MZ1 f2089 / SLZ1 f723 / LZ1 f5745 / LZ2 f1068 first-error frames unchanged. No frontier regressed.
- Next LZ3 blocker (NOT in this commit): f6517 LZ-conveyor (Obj 0x63) landing/carry ‚ÄĒ a separate root cluster of ~1780 ¬Ī1px conveyor-ride residuals; see memory `lz3-conveyor-and-angle-roots`.
## 2026-06-22 - S2 CPU sidekick push-bypass auto-jump re-trigger advances HTZ2 to f3315

- Branch/worktree context: `bugfix/ai-htz2-f1343-tails-autojump` (worktree off `develop`, base already carries the Rexon-stagger fix that put HTZ2 at f1343).
- Command: `mvn -q -Dmse=relaxed test "-Dtest=TestS2Htz2LevelSelectTraceReplay" -DfailIfNoTests=false "-Dsurefire.argLine=-Xmx6g -Xshare:off"`.
- First error before: f1343 `tails_x_speed` expected=0x0030 actual=0x003C. First error after: f3315 `tails_x_speed` expected=0x01E8 actual=0x00E8. Status: still red at the new (unrelated) frontier; HTZ2 advanced ~1972 frames, error count 1359 -> 1059.
- Root cause: at f1343 ROM's CPU Tails presses jump (`ctrl2_pressed=0x10` = button B) via the auto-jump trigger gate while grounded and pushing the HTZ breakable block (Obj32); `y_speed` becomes -0680 and Tails goes air+rolling. The engine kept Tails grounded (`gen=0008`, no jump). The engine's `Tails_CPU_jumping` latch (`jumpingFlag`) was stuck set from the prior `$3F`-cadence jump and was never cleared because the grounded-clear (ROM `loc_13E64` / S2 `FilterAction`) is skipped on the push-bypass path. The trigger gate was guarded by `if (!jumpingFlag)`, so the held latch blocked the legitimate re-trigger. ROM's push-bypass route branches **directly** to the trigger gate (`loc_13E9C` / `FilterAction_Part2`), which never consults the latch (S3K `loc_13DD0` sonic3k.asm:26702-26705; S2 s2.asm:39297-39300; gate s2.asm:39015-39022).
- Fix: `SidekickCpuController.updateNormal()` gate widened from `if (!jumpingFlag)` to `if (!jumpingFlag || autoJumpPushBypass)`. Shared S2/S3K CPU code, driven by real push state (no zone carve-out).
- Regression sweep (this branch vs clean develop baseline, first-error frame is the metric): all S2 traces identical first-error frame ‚ÄĒ ARZ2 f523, CPZ f3365, CPZ2 f2889, DEZ f4007, MCZ2 f4485, MTZ f1267, MTZ2 f1265, OOZ f1782, OOZ2 f1070, HTZ1 f6114, MTZ3 f1973; CNZ1 f1691 and CNZ2 f4418 same first-error frame (downstream count only, 532->549 / 982->999); ARZ1/MCZ1/SCZ/WFZ/EHZ1 green both. S3K AIZ level-select f19089 and complete-run f1095 byte-identical to baseline. S1 credits GHZ1/MZ2 green. TestSidekickCpuFollowParity unchanged at its 2 pre-existing develop-baseline failures. No frontier regressed.
## 2026-06-22 - S3K MGZ/LBZ Smashing Pillar inclusive right edge advances LBZ1 to f2270
